items.title: README
items.text:

items.title: Build Notes
items.text:
• Generate the raw spec with make docs-openapi.
  • Build the full site with make docs.
  • Preview locally with make docs-serve.
  • Export the curated product-definition ZIP with make docs-export-product.
  • Live /openapi.json exists only when service-http is built with --features openapi.
  • Live /docs exists only when service-http is built with --features "openapi scalar-docs".

items.title: API cURL Examples
items.text: Examples in this page target the current v2 HTTP surface implemented by service-http. For the canonical route contract, see API Reference.

items.title: Health Check
items.text:
curl -i http://localhost:8080/v2/health

Expected status: 200 OK

items.title: Async
items.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/v2/c2pa/video \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "inputs": [
      {
        "type": "remote_file_url",
        "url": "https://example.com/video.mp4",
        "filename_hint": "video.mp4"
      }
    ],
    "params": {
      "output_name": "signed-video.mp4",
      "options": {
        "timeout_ms": 2500,
        "in_place": false
      }
    },
    "options": {
      "max_files_parallel": 2
    }
  }'

items.title: In-Request File Payload
items.text:
The request model also supports base64-embedded file inputs:

curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/v2/sync/c2pa/video \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "inputs": [
      {
        "type": "in_request",
        "media_type": "video/mp4",
        "data_base64": "AA==",
        "filename": "clip.mp4"
      }
    ],
    "params": {}
  }'

items.title: Request-Selected S3 Output
items.text:
Available only when the service is built with --features s3 and the target bucket is configured in output_sinks.s3.buckets.

curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/v2/c2pa/video \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "inputs": [
      {
        "type": "remote_file_url",
        "url": "s3://media-bucket/input/video.mp4",
        "filename_hint": "video.mp4"
      }
    ],
    "params": {
      "output_name": "signed-video.mp4"
    },
    "output": {
      "type": "s3",
      "bucket": "processed-media",
      "prefix": "exports/c2pa"
    }
  }'

items.title: Notes
items.text:
• Async responses do not include a status_url; poll GET /v2/jobs/{id}.
  • Sync endpoints return 408 if the job does not finish before server.sync_timeout_ms.
  • Omitting output uses the configured local sink.
  • There is no local_path request output type.

items.title: Build And Run
items.text:
cargo build -p service-http
cargo run -p service-http

Optional feature builds:

# Enable OpenAPI JSON at /openapi.json
cargo run -p service-http --features openapi

# Enable OpenAPI JSON and Scalar UI at /docs
cargo run -p service-http --features "openapi scalar-docs"

# Enable S3-backed inputs and request-selectable S3 outputs
cargo run -p service-http --features s3

items.title: Runtime Configuration
items.text:
The service loads config/service.toml by default.

RUST_LOG=info
SERVICE_CONFIG_PATH=config/service.toml
SERVICE__SERVER__BIND_ADDR=127.0.0.1:8080
SERVICE__SERVER__SYNC_TIMEOUT_MS=120000
SERVICE__SERVER__SYNC_POLL_INTERVAL_MS=250
SERVICE__SERVER__MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES=52428800

items.title: Endpoint Overview
items.text: Purpose Async Endpoint Sync Endpoint Accepted Inputs Sign monolithic video assets POST /v2/c2pa/video POST /v2/sync/c2pa/video file inputs Sign fragmented/HLS publications POST /v2/c2pa/fragmented POST /v2/sync/c2pa/fragmented folder inputs Package media publications POST /v2/package POST /v2/sync/package folder inputs Package and sign POST /v2/c2pa/package POST /v2/sync/c2pa/package folder inputs Poll async job state GET /v2/jobs/{id} - - Health probe GET /v2/health - - Softbinding placeholder POST /v2/watermark - not implemented Softbinding placeholder GET /v2/watermark/{id} - not implemented

items.title: Input Types
items.text:
Current request inputs are provider-agnostic:

{"type": "in_request", "media_type": "video/mp4", "data_base64": "AA==", "filename": "clip.mp4"}
{"type": "local_file_path", "path": "./tests/fixtures/video/mp4/video1.mp4"}
{"type": "local_folder_path", "path": "./tests/fixtures/hls/publication"}
{"type": "remote_file_url", "url": "https://example.com/video.mp4", "filename_hint": "video.mp4"}
{"type": "remote_folder_url", "url": "s3://media-bucket/publication/"}

Processor rules:

  • c2pa/video accepts file inputs only.
  • c2pa/fragmented, package, and c2pa/package accept folder inputs only.

items.title: Output Selection
items.text:
If output is omitted or null, artifacts are written under the configured local sink rooted at output_sinks.local.base_dir.

With the s3 feature enabled and [output_sinks.s3] configured, requests may select an S3 destination:

{
  "output": {
    "type": "s3",
    "bucket": "processed-media",
    "prefix": "exports/c2pa"
  }
}

Notes:

  • There is no request local_path output type.
  • Only buckets listed in output_sinks.s3.buckets are accepted.
  • prefix: "/" writes at the configured base_prefix root when one is set.

items.text: GET /v2/health returns HTTP 200 OK with an empty body.

items.title: Softbinding Placeholders
items.text: POST /v2/watermark and GET /v2/watermark/{id} currently return HTTP 501 Not Implemented with body Coming soon.

items.title: Errors
items.text: Code HTTP Status Meaning INVALID_INPUT_TYPE 400 Processor received the wrong input category VALIDATION_FAILED 400 Request or output selection failed validation JOB_NOT_FOUND 404 No job exists for the requested ID REQUEST_TIMEOUT 408 Sync endpoint timed out while polling PROCESSING_FAILED 500 The worker failed while resolving, processing, or writing outputs INTERNAL_ERROR 500 Unexpected server-side failure

items.title: Operational Notes
items.text:
• All processing endpoints accept batched requests via inputs, but the array must contain at least one input.
  • Sync endpoints poll until the job reaches a terminal state or server.sync_timeout_ms is exceeded.
  • Partial job completion is surfaced as status: "failed" in the HTTP response model.
  • Request body size is capped by server.max_upload_bytes.

items.title: API Reference
items.text: This page is the canonical human-written contract for the current service-http v2 API. Use it for behavior, wire shape, and feature-gated capability notes. Use API cURL Examples for runnable requests and openapi.json for the generated machine-readable specification built by make docs.

items.title: GET /v2/health
items.text:
• Purpose: liveness and readiness probe for the HTTP service
  • Success: 200 OK
  • Body: empty

items.title: GET /v2/jobs/{id}
items.text:
• Purpose: retrieve the current snapshot of an asynchronous or synchronous batch job
  • Success: 200 OK with job_id, top-level status, results, and timing
  • Errors:
  • 404 JOB_NOT_FOUND when the batch ID does not exist
  • 500 PROCESSING_FAILED or 500 INTERNAL_ERROR for unexpected service-side failures

items.title: POST /v2/c2pa/video
items.text:
• Purpose: submit monolithic video signing as an asynchronous job
  • Accepted inputs: file inputs only
  • in_request
  • local_file_path
  • remote_file_url
  • Request fields:
  • inputs: required, one or more file inputs
  • params.output_name: optional
  • params.options.timeout_ms: optional
  • params.options.in_place: optional
  • output: optional; omitted means configured local sink
  • options.max_files_parallel: optional
  • Success: 200 OK with job_id and status = "queued"
  • Errors:
  • 400 INVALID_INPUT_TYPE for folder-style inputs
  • 400 VALIDATION_FAILED for malformed or disallowed payloads
  • 500 PROCESSING_FAILED or 500 INTERNAL_ERROR

items.title: POST /v2/sync/c2pa/video
items.text:
• Purpose: submit monolithic video signing and poll until completion
  • Accepted inputs: same as async endpoint
  • Success: 200 OK with final JobResultResponse
  • Timeout: 408 REQUEST_TIMEOUT when the job does not finish within server.sync_timeout_ms
  • Errors: same validation and server-side failure model as the async endpoint

items.title: POST /v2/c2pa/fragmented
items.text:
• Purpose: submit fragmented/HLS signing as an asynchronous job
  • Accepted inputs: folder inputs only
  • local_folder_path
  • remote_folder_url
  • Required params:
  • publication_name
  • playlist_pattern
  • init_pattern
  • frag_pattern
  • Success: 200 OK with queued job response
  • Errors:
  • 400 INVALID_INPUT_TYPE for file inputs
  • 400 VALIDATION_FAILED
  • 500 PROCESSING_FAILED or 500 INTERNAL_ERROR

items.title: POST /v2/sync/c2pa/fragmented
items.text:
• Purpose: submit fragmented/HLS signing and wait for completion
  • Accepted inputs: same as async endpoint
  • Success: 200 OK with final JobResultResponse
  • Timeout: 408 REQUEST_TIMEOUT
  • Errors: same validation and server-side failure model as the async endpoint

items.title: POST /v2/package
items.text:
• Purpose: submit packaging-only processing as an asynchronous job
  • Accepted inputs: folder inputs only
  • local_folder_path
  • remote_folder_url
  • Required params:
  • output_name
  • Success: 200 OK with queued job response
  • Errors:
  • 400 INVALID_INPUT_TYPE
  • 400 VALIDATION_FAILED
  • 500 PROCESSING_FAILED or 500 INTERNAL_ERROR

items.title: POST /v2/sync/package
items.text:
• Purpose: package media and wait for terminal job state
  • Accepted inputs: same as async endpoint
  • Success: 200 OK with final JobResultResponse
  • Timeout: 408 REQUEST_TIMEOUT
  • Errors: same validation and server-side failure model as the async endpoint

items.title: POST /v2/c2pa/package
items.text:
• Purpose: package media and apply C2PA signing in one asynchronous job
  • Accepted inputs: folder inputs only
  • local_folder_path
  • remote_folder_url
  • Required params:
  • output_name
  • Optional params:
  • playlist_pattern
  • init_pattern
  • frag_pattern
  • Success: 200 OK with queued job response
  • Errors:
  • 400 INVALID_INPUT_TYPE
  • 400 VALIDATION_FAILED
  • 500 PROCESSING_FAILED or 500 INTERNAL_ERROR

items.title: POST /v2/sync/c2pa/package
items.text:
• Purpose: package and sign while waiting for completion
  • Accepted inputs: same as async endpoint
  • Success: 200 OK with final JobResultResponse
  • Timeout: 408 REQUEST_TIMEOUT
  • Errors: same validation and server-side failure model as the async endpoint

items.title: Output Selection
items.text:
• If output is omitted or null, artifacts go to the configured local sink rooted at output_sinks.local.base_dir.
  • output.type = "s3" is available only when the service is built with --features s3 and the requested bucket is present in output_sinks.s3.buckets.
  • There is no supported request output type named local_path.

items.title: Error Model
items.text: Code HTTP Status Meaning INVALID_INPUT_TYPE 400 Processor received a mismatched input category VALIDATION_FAILED 400 Request fields or selected output failed validation JOB_NOT_FOUND 404 The requested batch ID does not exist REQUEST_TIMEOUT 408 Sync polling exceeded server.sync_timeout_ms PROCESSING_FAILED 500 Job execution failed while resolving, processing, or writing outputs INTERNAL_ERROR 500 Unexpected internal service failure

items.title: Overview
items.text: This document describes the organization and architecture of the c2pa-claim-generator workspace.

items.title: Workspace Structure
items.text:
c2pa-claim-generator/
├── crates/
    ├── domain/          # Core business logic & traits (no external deps)
    ├── app/             # Application orchestration layer
    ├── infra/           # Infrastructure implementations
    └── service-http/    # HTTP API layer (Axum)

items.title: Dependency Graph
items.text:
service-http
    ↓
  app  ←──────┐
    ↓         │
  infra ──────┤
    ↓         │
 domain ←─────┘

media-packager (standalone, external repository, used by infra)

items.title: 1. Clean Dependency Inversion (Ports & Adapters)
items.text:
• domain defines traits (Processor, JobStore, InputResolvers, OutputSink)
  • infra provides concrete implementations
  • app orchestrates without knowing implementation details
  • Follows hexagonal architecture principles

items.title: 2. Type-Safe Processor System
items.text:
• Generic Processor<Input> trait with associated types
  • Type erasure via DynProcessor for dynamic dispatch
  • Clean separation: processors in infra, registry in app

items.title: 3. Feature Flags for Resolvers
items.text:
• infra exposes local, http, s3, etc. features
  • service-http selects only what it needs
  • Reduces binary size and dependencies

items.title: domain
items.text:
Purpose: Core business logic, traits, and domain models

Contains: - Processor trait and base types - Job management abstractions (JobStore, BatchService) - Input/output abstractions (InputResolver, OutputSink) - Domain models (BatchSpec, ProcessedArtifact, etc.)

Dependencies: Minimal—only serialization and async traits

items.title: app
items.text:
Purpose: Application orchestration and runtime

Contains: - BatchService implementation - Worker pool management - Processor registry - Job execution logic

Dependencies: domain, infra

items.title: infra
items.text:
Purpose: Concrete implementations of domain ports

Contains: - Processors: C2PA signing, media packaging - Input resolvers: local filesystem, HTTP, S3 - Output sinks: local filesystem - Job stores: in-memory, (future: SQLite)

Dependencies: domain, external libraries (c2pa, media-packager, reqwest, etc.)

Features: - local: Local filesystem support - http: HTTP input resolver - s3: AWS S3 input resolver support

Remote object-storage inputs stay provider-agnostic in domain. The HTTP API accepts remote_file_url and remote_folder_url, and infra dispatches by URL scheme to the appropriate resolver implementation.

items.title: service-http
items.text:
Purpose: HTTP API layer using Axum

Contains: - REST API endpoints - Request/response DTOs - HTTP-specific middleware - Server configuration

Dependencies: app, domain, infra, axum

items.title: Stability Guarantees
items.text:
Stable API (1.x+): - domain::Processor trait - domain::BatchService - domain::JobStore, domain::InputResolver, domain::OutputSink - Core models: BatchSpec, InputRef, OutputRef, ProcessedArtifact

Experimental (may change in minor versions): - Resolved input types (ResolvedMediaInput, etc.) - Media-specific processors

items.title: Organization Principles
items.text:
1. Root re-exports for common types: Frequently used stable types
  2. Module organization by category: processors, stores, resolvers, sinks
  3. Presets module: "Batteries included" factories for common configurations
  4. Visibility control: pub(crate) for workspace-internal items

items.title: Testing Strategy
items.text:
• Unit tests: Within each crate (#[cfg(test)] modules)
  • Integration tests: In tests/integration/
  • End-to-end tests: In tests/e2e/
  • Benchmarks: In benches/
  • Test fixtures: In tests/fixtures/

items.title: Debug Build
items.text: cargo build

items.title: Release Build
items.text: cargo build --release

items.title: Running Tests
items.text: cargo test --workspace

items.title: Running Service
items.text: cargo run -p service-http

items.title: System Design
items.text: The c2pa-claim-generator is a high-performance, async batch processing service for C2PA signing and media packaging operations. It follows a hexagonal architecture (ports and adapters) pattern with clear separation of concerns.

items.title: Core Principles
items.text:
1. Dependency Inversion: Domain defines contracts, infrastructure provides implementations
  2. Type Safety: Compile-time guarantees through Rust's type system
  3. Async-First: Built on Tokio for concurrent processing
  4. Extensibility: Trait-based abstractions allow easy addition of new processors
  5. Configuration-Driven: Runtime behavior controlled through configuration files

items.title: domain
items.text:
Responsibility: Core business logic and abstractions

  • Defines all traits (Processor, InputResolver, OutputSink, JobStore)
  • Domain models (BatchSpec, InputRef, ProcessedArtifact, etc.)
  • No external dependencies except serde and async-trait
  • Stability: This is the stable 1.x+ API surface

items.title: app
items.text:
Responsibility: Application orchestration

  • BatchService implementation
  • Worker pool management
  • Job queue and lifecycle management
  • ProcessorRegistry
  • Coordinates domain and infra layers

items.title: infra
items.text:
Responsibility: Concrete implementations

  • Processors: C2PA signing (monolithic and fragmented), media packaging, combined operations
  • Resolvers: Local filesystem, HTTP downloads, S3
  • Stores: In-memory job storage, (future: SQLite/PostgreSQL)
  • Sinks: Local filesystem output, (future: S3, GCS)

Feature flags: - local - Local filesystem support - http - HTTP input resolver - s3 - AWS S3 input resolver support

items.title: service-http
items.text:
Responsibility: HTTP API layer

  • Axum-based REST API
  • Request/response DTOs
  • Validation using garde
  • Endpoint handlers
  • Sync and async endpoint modes

items.title: 1. Request Ingestion
items.text: HTTP Request → Validation → DTO Conversion → BatchSpec

items.title: 2. Job Creation
items.text: BatchSpec → BatchService → JobStore → Job ID

items.title: 3. Worker Processing
items.text: Worker Pool → Dequeue Job → Resolve Inputs → Process → Write Output

items.title: 4. Resolution Phase
items.text: InputRef → InputResolver → ResolvedInput (typed per processor)

items.title: 5. Processing Phase
items.text: ResolvedInput → Processor<Input> → ProcessedArtifact

items.title: 6. Output Phase
items.text: ProcessedArtifact → OutputSink → OutputRef

items.title: Type-Safe Processing
items.text:
Each processor declares its input type:

trait Processor {
    type Input;
    async fn process(&self, input: Self::Input, ctx: ProcessContext) 
        -> Result<ProcessedArtifact>;
}

Examples: - C2paMonolithicProcessor: Processor<Input = ResolvedFileInput> - C2paFmp4Processor: Processor<Input = ResolvedHlsPublication> - MediaPackagerProcessor: Processor<Input = ResolvedFolderInput>

items.title: Processor Registry
items.text: Dynamic dispatch through DynProcessor trait objects stored in a registry keyed by processor kind.

items.title: Worker Pool
items.text:
• Configurable number of workers (default: CPU cores)
  • Each worker runs an infinite loop processing jobs from a queue
  • Tokio-based async concurrency

items.title: Per-Job Parallelism
items.text:
• Each job can process multiple inputs in parallel
  • Controlled by max_files_parallel option (default: 4)
  • Futures spawned with semaphore-based rate limiting

items.title: Sync vs Async Endpoints
items.text:
• Async endpoints: Return job ID immediately, client polls
  • Sync endpoints: Block and poll internally until completion or timeout

items.title: Error Hierarchy
items.text:
ProcessorError (domain)
    ├── InputResolutionError
    ├── ProcessingError
    └── OutputSinkError

MediaPackagerError (media-packager)
    ├── CommandNotFound
    ├── CommandFailed
    └── InvalidConfig

C2paError (from c2pa crate)

items.title: Error Propagation
items.text:
• Errors wrapped in anyhow::Error for flexibility
  • Per-input error tracking in job results
  • Fail-fast for fatal errors, continue for input-specific failures

items.title: Runtime Configuration Files
items.text: Located in config/: - service.toml: Top-level server, worker, resolver, sink, and processor path settings - c2pa/: C2PA SDK settings, signer configurations - media-packager/: Pipeline configurations, representation metadata - signers/: Remote signer endpoints and credentials - keys/: Public keys and certificates

  • SERVICE_CONFIG_PATH - Alternate top-level service config file
  • SERVICE__... - Overrides for values in config/service.toml

items.text:

  • Sync mode: Depends on processing time + polling overhead

  • CPU: Scales with parallelism settings
  • Disk: Temporary files cleaned up automatically

items.title: Overview
items.text:
Refactored the app crate to use thiserror for structured, type-safe error handling that provides clear error boundaries between application orchestration layer and domain/infrastructure layers.

Date: March 9, 2026 Scope: crates/app/ Architecture: Hexagonal (Ports & Adapters)

items.title: Goals Achieved
items.text:
1. ✅ Type-safe error handling using thiserror derive macros
  2. ✅ Clear error boundaries - distinct error types for different concerns
  3. ✅ Rich context tracking - full error chains via #[source] attribute
  4. ✅ Hexagonal architecture compliance - errors respect layer boundaries
  5. ✅ Automatic error propagation - leveraging ? operator and From traits
  6. ✅ Improved debugging - structured error context for logs and traces

items.title: Error Type Hierarchy
items.text:
pub enum AppError {
    JobSubmission { source: JobError },      // Job submission failed
    JobStatusQuery { source: JobError },     // Status retrieval failed
    JobProcessing { source: JobError },      // Job processing failed
    Registry { source: RegistryError },      // Processor registry error
    Configuration { message, source },       // Configuration error
    JobStore { source: anyhow::Error },      // Job store operation failed
    Channel { source: Box<dyn Error> },      // Channel communication failed
}

pub enum RegistryError {
    ProcessorNotFound { kind: ProcessorKind },    // Processor not registered
    ConfigurationFile { path, source },           // Config file error
    ProcessorInitialization { kind, source },     // Processor init failed
}

pub enum JobError {
    ValidationFailed { source },        // Batch spec validation failed
    NotFound { job_id },                // Job not found in store
    StoreFailed { source },             // Job store operation failed
    InputResolution { source },         // Input resolution failed
    ProcessorExecution { source },      // Processor execution failed
    OutputSink { source },              // Output sink operation failed
    SemaphoreAcquisition,               // Internal sync error
    EnqueueFailed { source },           // Channel enqueue failed
    UnsupportedArtifact { artifact_type }, // Unsupported output type
}

items.title: 1. Separation of Concerns
items.text: Each error type addresses a specific concern: - JobError: Job lifecycle operations (validation, execution, storage) - RegistryError: Processor discovery and initialization - AppError: Top-level application orchestration

items.title: 2. Source Error Tracking
items.text:
All errors preserve source context via #[source]:

#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum JobError {
    #[error("Processor execution failed")]
    ProcessorExecution {
        #[source]
        source: anyhow::Error,  // Full error chain preserved
    },
    // ...
}

items.title: 3. Semantic Constructors
items.text:
Helper methods provide semantic clarity:

impl JobError {
    pub fn processor_execution(err: anyhow::Error) -> Self {
        JobError::ProcessorExecution { source: err }
    }

    pub fn not_found(job_id: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        JobError::NotFound { job_id: job_id.into() }
    }
}

items.title: 4. Layer-Appropriate Error Handling
items.text:
Each layer handles only its concerns:

// Runner (app layer) - converts infrastructure errors to JobError
let artifact = processor
    .process_boxed(input, ctx, resolvers)
    .await
    .map_err(JobError::processor_execution)?;

// BatchService - converts JobError to AppError with context
let id = self.job_store
    .create_job(spec)
    .await
    .map_err(|e| AppError::JobSubmission {
        source: JobError::store(e),
    })?;

items.title: 1. batch_service.rs
items.text:
Before: 

async fn submit(&self, spec: BatchSpec) -> anyhow::Result<BatchId> {
    validate_spec(&spec)?;
    let id = self.job_store.create_job(spec).await?;
    self.sender.send(id.clone()).await?;
    Ok(id)
}

After: 

async fn submit(&self, spec: BatchSpec) -> Result<BatchId, AppError> {
    validate_spec(&spec).map_err(|e| AppError::JobSubmission {
        source: JobError::validation(e),
    })?;

    let id = self.job_store
        .create_job(spec)
        .await
        .map_err(|e| AppError::JobSubmission {
            source: JobError::store(e),
        })?;

    self.sender
        .send(id.clone())
        .await
        .map_err(|e| AppError::JobSubmission {
            source: JobError::EnqueueFailed { source: Box::new(e) },
        })?;

    Ok(id)
}

Benefits: - Clear error categorization (validation vs store vs channel) - Rich context for debugging - Type-safe error handling

items.title: 2. runner.rs
items.text:
Before: 

async fn handle_job(
    batch_id: BatchId,
    deps: WorkerDeps,
    config: WorkerConfig,
) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    let snapshot = deps.job_store.get_job(&batch_id).await?;
    let processor = deps.processor_registry.get(&snapshot.spec.processor)?;
    // ...
}

After: 

async fn handle_job(
    batch_id: BatchId,
    deps: WorkerDeps,
    config: WorkerConfig,
) -> Result<(), JobError> {
    let snapshot = deps.job_store
        .get_job(&batch_id)
        .await
        .map_err(JobError::store)?;

    let processor = deps.processor_registry
        .get(&snapshot.spec.processor)
        .map_err(|e| JobError::ProcessorExecution {
            source: anyhow::anyhow!("Processor not found: {}", e),
        })?;
    // ...
}

Benefits: - Explicit error type (JobError instead of generic anyhow) - Structured logging with error source tracking - Better error categorization for metrics

items.title: 3. registry/mod.rs & map_registry.rs
items.text:
Before: 

pub trait ProcessorRegistry: Send + Sync {
    fn get(&self, spec: &ProcessorSpec) -> anyhow::Result<Arc<dyn DynProcessor>>;
}

impl ProcessorRegistry for MapProcessorRegistry {
    fn get(&self, spec: &ProcessorSpec) -> Result<Arc<dyn DynProcessor>, anyhow::Error> {
        self.map
            .get(&spec.kind)
            .cloned()
            .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("Unknown processor kind: {:?}", spec.kind))
    }
}

After: 

pub trait ProcessorRegistry: Send + Sync {
    fn get(&self, spec: &ProcessorSpec) -> Result<Arc<dyn DynProcessor>, RegistryError>;
}

impl ProcessorRegistry for MapProcessorRegistry {
    fn get(&self, spec: &ProcessorSpec) -> Result<Arc<dyn DynProcessor>, RegistryError> {
        self.map
            .get(&spec.kind)
            .cloned()
            .ok_or_else(|| RegistryError::processor_not_found(spec.kind))
    }
}

Benefits: - Type-safe error with ProcessorKind context - Clear error message: "Unknown processor kind: C2paSign" - Easier to handle specific error cases

items.title: 4. registry/default_registry.rs
items.text:
Before: 

pub fn build_processor_registry_with_config(
    c2pa_config_path: &str,
    media_packager_config_path: &str,
) -> anyhow::Result<MapProcessorRegistry> {
    let fragmented_c2pa_signer = Arc::new(
        C2pafMP4Processor::from_sdk_toml(c2pa_config_path)?
    );
    // ...
}

After: 

pub fn build_processor_registry_with_config(
    c2pa_config_path: &str,
    media_packager_config_path: &str,
) -> Result<MapProcessorRegistry, RegistryError> {
    let fragmented_c2pa_signer = Arc::new(
        C2pafMP4Processor::from_sdk_toml(c2pa_config_path)
            .map_err(|e| RegistryError::config_file(c2pa_config_path, e))?
    );
    // ...
}

Benefits: - Configuration path included in error message - Clear distinction between config file errors and init errors - Structured error for better diagnostics

items.title: Scenario: Processor Not Found
items.text:
1. HTTP Request → service-http
   ↓
2. BatchService.submit() → app
   ↓
3. ProcessorRegistry.get() → app/registry
   ↓
4. RegistryError::ProcessorNotFound { kind: C2paVideo }
   ↓
5. AppError::Registry { source: RegistryError }
   ↓
6. ApiError::internal("Processor registry error: Unknown processor kind: C2paVideo")
   ↓
7. HTTP 500 Response:
   {
     "error": {
       "code": "INTERNAL_ERROR",
       "message": "An internal error occurred. Please try again later."
     }
   }

8. Logs (with full context):
   ERROR processor registry error: Unknown processor kind: C2paVideo
         error_chain=RegistryError::ProcessorNotFound

items.title: Integration with service-http
items.text:
The service-http layer automatically converts AppError to ApiError:

impl From<app::AppError> for ApiError {
    fn from(err: app::AppError) -> Self {
        use app::AppError;
        match err {
            AppError::JobSubmission { source } | AppError::JobProcessing { source } => {
                match source {
                    JobError::ValidationFailed { .. } => 
                        Self::validation_failed(...),
                    JobError::NotFound { job_id } => 
                        Self::job_not_found(job_id),
                    JobError::StoreFailed { source } => 
                        Self::processing_failed(source),
                    // ... map all variants
                }
            }
            AppError::JobStatusQuery { source } => { /* ... */ }
            AppError::Registry { source } => 
                Self::internal(anyhow::anyhow!("Processor registry error: {}", source)),
            // ... handle all variants
        }
    }
}

This enables clean handler code:

pub async fn submit_job_async(
    state: &AppState,
    spec: BatchSpec,
) -> Result<Json<JobResponse>, ApiError> {
    let batch_id = state.service.submit(spec).await?;  // Automatic conversion!
    Ok(Json(JobResponse { /* ... */ }))
}

items.title: 1. Error Granularity
items.text: Each error variant represents a distinct failure mode: - JobError::ValidationFailed vs JobError::ProcessorExecution - RegistryError::ProcessorNotFound vs RegistryError::ConfigurationFile

items.title: 2. Context Preservation
items.text:
All errors preserve source context: 

#[error("Processor execution failed")]
ProcessorExecution {
    #[source]
    source: anyhow::Error,  // Full chain available
}

items.title: 3. Semantic Constructors
items.text:
Helper methods improve readability: 

// Instead of:
JobError::StoreFailed { source: err }

// Use:
JobError::store(err)

items.title: 4. Display Messages
items.text:
Error messages are human-readable and actionable: 

#[error("Job not found: {job_id}")]
NotFound { job_id: String }

// Produces: "Job not found: batch-abc-123"

items.title: 5. Logging Integration
items.text:
Errors include rich context for logging: 

error!(
    error = %err,
    error_source = ?err.source(),
    "batch processing failed"
);

items.title: Unit Tests
items.text:
#[test]
fn test_registry_error_processor_not_found() {
    let err = RegistryError::processor_not_found(ProcessorKind::C2paVideo);
    assert_eq!(err.to_string(), "Unknown processor kind: C2paVideo");
}

#[test]
fn test_job_error_source_tracking() {
    let source = anyhow::anyhow!("database connection failed");
    let err = JobError::store(source);

    assert!(matches!(err, JobError::StoreFailed { .. }));
    assert!(err.source().is_some());
}

items.text:
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_job_submission_with_invalid_spec() {
    let service = BatchApp::new(job_store, sender);
    let invalid_spec = BatchSpec { /* invalid data */ };

    let result = service.submit(invalid_spec).await;

    assert!(result.is_err());
    match result.unwrap_err() {
        AppError::JobSubmission { source: JobError::ValidationFailed { .. } } => {
            // Expected error type
        }
        _ => panic!("Expected validation error"),
    }
}

items.text:
• Add thiserror to app/Cargo.toml

  • Update ProcessorRegistry trait to return RegistryError
  • Refactor batch_service.rs error handling
  • Refactor runner.rs error handling
  • Refactor registry/mod.rs error handling
  • Refactor registry/map_registry.rs error handling
  • Refactor registry/default_registry.rs error handling

  • Add integration tests for error flows
  • Update API documentation

items.title: Before Refactor
items.text:
• ❌ Generic anyhow::Result at all layers
  • ❌ Lost error context during conversion
  • ❌ Difficult to pattern match on error types
  • ❌ Generic error messages leaked to API
  • ❌ Hard to track error origins

items.title: After Refactor
items.text:
• ✅ Type-safe, layer-specific error types
  • ✅ Full error chain preserved via #[source]
  • ✅ Easy pattern matching on error variants
  • ✅ Safe, generic messages for API responses
  • ✅ Clear error categorization for logging/metrics
  • ✅ Semantic constructors improve readability
  • ✅ Automatic error conversion via From traits
  • ✅ Hexagonal architecture boundaries respected

metrics::counter!("app.errors.job_submission").increment(1);

items.title: 2. Error Recovery
items.text:
impl AppError {
    pub fn is_retryable(&self) -> bool {
        matches!(self, AppError::Channel { .. } | AppError::JobStore { .. })
    }
}

items.title: 3. Error Codes
items.text:
impl RegistryError {
    pub fn code(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Self::ProcessorNotFound { .. } => "REG_001",
            Self::ConfigurationFile { .. } => "REG_002",
            Self::ProcessorInitialization { .. } => "REG_003",
        }
    }
}

items.title: Conclusion
items.text:
The app crate now uses structured, type-safe error handling that: - Respects hexagonal architecture boundaries - Provides rich context for debugging - Enables pattern matching on error types - Integrates seamlessly with service-http layer - Follows Rust error handling best practices

All error flows are explicit, traceable, and type-checked at compile time.

items.title: Current Structure Overview
items.text:
c2pa-claim-generator/
├── domain/          # Core business logic & traits (no external deps)
├── app/             # Application orchestration layer
├── infra/           # Infrastructure implementations
├── media-packager/  # Standalone media processing library

items.title: Dependency Graph
items.text:
service-http
    ↓
  app  ←──────┐
    ↓         │
  infra ──────┤
    ↓         │
 domain ←─────┘

media-packager (standalone, used by infra)

items.title: 1. Clean Dependency Inversion (Ports & Adapters)
items.text:
• domain defines traits (Processor, JobStore, InputResolvers, OutputSink)
  • infra provides concrete implementations
  • app orchestrates without knowing implementation details
  • Follows hexagonal architecture principles well

items.title: 2. media-packager as Independent Library
items.text:
• Zero dependency on domain/app/infra
  • Can be extracted to a separate repo easily
  • Well-documented with examples
  • Good public API design with clear exports
  • Self-contained with FFmpeg/Bento4 abstractions

items.title: 3. Type-Safe Processor System
items.text:
• Generic Processor<Input> trait with associated types
  • Type erasure via DynProcessor for dynamic dispatch
  • Clean separation: processors in infra, registry in app

items.title: 4. Feature Flags for Resolvers
items.text:
• infra exposes local, http, s3, etc. features
  • service-http selects only what it needs
  • Reduces binary size and dependencies

items.title: 1. infra → media-packager Dependency Violation
items.text:
Problem: 

# infra/Cargo.toml
[dependencies.media-packager]
path = "../media-packager"
- infra depends on media-packager for MediaPackagerProcessor - media-packager is supposed to be standalone - Creates coupling: changes in media-packager force infra rebuild

Impact: - Violates "library should be independent" goal - PackageAndSignProcessor in infra directly uses media-packager types

Why it happened: - MediaPackagerProcessor is a domain Processor implementation - But it wraps a library (media-packager::MediaPipeline) that knows nothing about domain

Fix Options: 1. Move MediaPackagerProcessor into media-packager itself as an adapter module 2. Create a thin adapter in infra that re-exports only what's needed 3. Keep as-is but document this as intentional (least clean)

items.title: 2. Scattered Public API Definitions
items.text:
Problem: - domain/src/lib.rs re-exports 21 items from 6 modules - No clear "these are stable, these are internal" markers - Models mixed with traits mixed with utilities

Example: 

pub use models::{
    BatchId, BatchMetrics, BatchSnapshot, ByteStream, FileResult, 
    FileStatus, JobStatus, MediaInput, OutputRef, ResolvedFileInput, 
    ResolvedFolderInput, ResolvedMediaInput, SeekableReadHandle,
};
All exported at the same level—no signal about stability.

Fix: - Group re-exports by category (traits, models, utilities) - Add #[doc(hidden)] for internal-but-public items - Consider pub(crate) for items only used within workspace

items.title: 3. app Crate is Thin (Maybe Too Thin?)
items.text:
Current State: 

// app/src/lib.rs
pub mod batch_service;
pub mod registry;
pub mod runner;
- Only 3 modules, minimal logic - Most orchestration happens in service-http/src/main.rs

Questions: 1. Should WorkerConfig, job_queue(), spawn_worker_loop() be in app or service-http? - Currently: app (good—reusable for CLI or other transports) 2. Should build_processor_registry() be in app or service-http? - Currently: service-http/src/processing.rs (bad—not reusable)

Recommendation: - Move build_processor_registry() into app as a default factory - service-http should only contain HTTP-specific code (routes, handlers, DTOs)

items.title: 4. infra Public API is Flat
items.text:
Current: 

// infra/src/lib.rs
pub use c2pa::{C2paProcessor, C2pafMP4Processor};
pub use media_packager::{MediaPackagerProcessor, PackageAndSignProcessor};
pub use resolvers::CompositeInputResolver;
pub use sinks::SimpleOutputSinkFactory;
pub use stores::{InMemoryJobStore, SqliteJobStore};

Issues: - All implementations exposed at root level - No namespace grouping (users write infra::C2paProcessor instead of infra::processors::C2pa) - Hard to see which items are "batteries included" vs. advanced

Better API: 

// Batteries-included presets
pub use presets::{default_resolvers, default_sink, default_store};

// Organized by category
pub mod processors {
    pub use crate::c2pa::{C2paProcessor, C2pafMP4Processor};
    pub use crate::media_packager::{MediaPackagerProcessor, PackageAndSignProcessor};
}
pub mod resolvers { /* ... */ }
pub mod stores { /* ... */ }
pub mod sinks { /* ... */ }

items.title: 5. domain Models Could Use More Visibility Control
items.text:
Current: 

// domain/src/lib.rs
pub mod models;  // Everything inside is pub

Problem: - models/mod.rs likely has many internal types that leak into public API - Users might depend on internal structs

Fix: - Keep models private: mod models; - Re-export only stable types: pub use models::{BatchId, BatchSnapshot, ...};

items.title: 6. Missing Workspace-Level Public API Validation
items.text:
No tracking of: - What is "stable 1.x API" vs. experimental - Breaking changes across versions - Public API surface size

Recommendation: - Add cargo-public-api to CI (already mentioned in mode instructions) - Document stability guarantees in crate-level docs

items.title: Priority 1: Fix infra → media-packager Coupling
items.text:
Option A (Recommended): Create adapter module in media-packager 

// media-packager/src/domain_adapter.rs (feature-gated)
#[cfg(feature = "domain-integration")]
pub mod domain_adapter {
    use super::*;

Current:

    BatchId, BatchMetrics, BatchSnapshot, ByteStream, FileResult, FileStatus, 
    JobStatus, MediaInput, OutputRef, ResolvedFileInput, ResolvedFolderInput,

Questions to ask: 1. Does service-http need ByteStream directly? → Probably not (internal) 2. Does external code need SeekableReadHandle? → No (implementation detail) 3. Should FileResult/FileStatus be public? → Maybe only through BatchSnapshot

items.title: Architecture Maturity Assessment
items.text: Aspect Rating Notes Dependency Inversion ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Excellent—domain defines contracts Separation of Concerns ⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ Good, but app is thin Public API Clarity ⭐⭐⭐☆☆ Too many exports, no stability markers Module Organization ⭐⭐⭐☆☆ Flat namespaces in infra Library Independence ⭐⭐⭐☆☆ media-packager coupled to infra Testability ⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ Traits make mocking easy Documentation ⭐⭐⭐☆☆ Code-level docs good, crate-level sparse

items.title: Similar to:
items.text:
• Actix Web ecosystem: Clean trait-based abstractions
  • Tokio: Separate tokio (core) from tokio-util (helpers)
  • Tower: tower (traits) + tower-http (implementations)

items.title: Different from:
items.text:
• Axum: More monolithic (but they're a framework, not a library)
  • Diesel: More macro-heavy, less runtime polymorphism

items.title: Final Verdict
items.text:
Overall: 7.5/10 🎯

Strengths: - Solid hexagonal architecture foundation - Type-safe processor system with good ergonomics - media-packager is well-designed as a library

Main Gaps: 1. infra → media-packager coupling breaks library independence 2. Public API lacks organization and stability markers 3. app crate is underutilized (registry building should move there)

If you fix Priority 1-3, this becomes a 9/10 architecture—production-ready and maintainable.

items.title: Overview
items.text:
Refactored the service-http crate to use thiserror for structured, type-safe error handling that provides predictable error messages without leaking internal implementation details.

Date: March 9, 2026 Scope: crates/service-http/src/v2/

items.title: Goals Achieved
items.text:
1. ✅ Type-safe error handling using thiserror derive macros
  2. ✅ Separation of concerns - internal errors tracked separately from user-facing messages
  3. ✅ No information leakage - sensitive details logged but not exposed to API clients
  4. ✅ Automatic error logging - internal errors logged with full context
  5. ✅ Reduced boilerplate - leveraging From trait implementations
  6. ✅ Consistent error responses - uniform JSON structure across all endpoints

items.title: Error Type Hierarchy
items.text:
pub enum ApiError {
    InvalidInput { message, source },          // 400 - Client provided invalid input
    InvalidInputType { message, input_index, source }, // 400 - Wrong input type for processor
    ValidationFailed { message, source },      // 400 - Schema/constraint validation failed
    JobNotFound { job_id },                    // 404 - Job ID doesn't exist
    Timeout,                                   // 408 - Sync request exceeded timeout
    ProcessingFailed { source },               // 500 - Job submission/processing error
    Internal { source },                       // 500 - Unexpected internal error
}

items.title: 1. Source Tracking with #[source]
items.text:
Internal errors use #[source] attribute to maintain error chains for debugging:

#[error("Processing failed")]
ProcessingFailed {
    #[source]
    source: anyhow::Error,  // Full error chain preserved
}

items.title: 2. Safe User Messages
items.text:
The user_message() method provides sanitized, generic messages for API responses:

fn user_message(&self) -> String {
    match self {
        Self::Internal { .. } => {
            "An internal error occurred. Please try again later.".to_string()
        }
        Self::ProcessingFailed { .. } => {
            "Processing failed. Please check your request and try again.".to_string()
        }
        // ... specific messages for client errors
    }
}

items.title: 3. Automatic Error Logging
items.text:
Internal errors are automatically logged with full context in IntoResponse:

match &self {
    Self::Internal { source } => {
        error!(
            error = %source,
            error_chain = ?source,
            "Internal server error"
        );
    }
    // ...
}

items.title: 4. Automatic Conversions via From
items.text:
Common error types auto-convert to ApiError:

impl From<anyhow::Error> for ApiError
impl From<garde::Report> for ApiError
impl From<serde_json::Error> for ApiError

This enables clean handler code: 

// Before
req.validate()
    .map_err(|e| ApiError::validation_failed(e.to_string()))?;

// After
req.validate()?;  // Automatic conversion via From<garde::Report>

items.title: API Response Format
items.text:
All errors return a consistent JSON structure:

{
  "error": {
    "code": "VALIDATION_FAILED",
    "message": "Validation failed: inputs must not be empty",
    "details": {
      "input_index": 0,
      "field": "path"
    }
  }
}

items.title: Error Codes
items.text: Code HTTP Status Description INVALID_INPUT 400 Client provided invalid input format INVALID_INPUT_TYPE 400 Input type doesn't match processor requirements VALIDATION_FAILED 400 Request failed schema/constraint validation JOB_NOT_FOUND 404 Job ID not found in store REQUEST_TIMEOUT 408 Sync request exceeded configured timeout PROCESSING_FAILED 500 Job submission or processing error INTERNAL_ERROR 500 Unexpected internal error

items.title: Before Refactor
items.text:
pub async fn c2pa_video_async(
    State(state): State<AppState>,
    Json(req): Json<C2paVideoRequest>,
) -> Result<Json<JobResponse>, ApiError> {
    // Verbose error mapping
    req.validate()
        .map_err(|e| ApiError::validation_failed(e.to_string()))?;

    VideoInputValidator.validate_inputs(&req.inputs)?;

    let spec = req
        .try_into()
        .map_err(|e: serde_json::Error| ApiError::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;

    submit_job_async(&state, spec).await
}

items.title: After Refactor
items.text:
pub async fn c2pa_video_async(
    State(state): State<AppState>,
    Json(req): Json<C2paVideoRequest>,
) -> Result<Json<JobResponse>, ApiError> {
    // Clean, automatic error conversions
    req.validate()?;
    VideoInputValidator.validate_inputs(&req.inputs)?;
    let spec = req.try_into()?;
    submit_job_async(&state, spec).await
}

items.title: Information Leakage Prevention
items.text:
Before: Error messages exposed internal details 

ApiError::internal_error(format!("Database connection failed: {}", db_error))
// Response: "Database connection failed: Connection refused at localhost:5432"

After: Generic messages, details only in logs 

ApiError::internal(db_error.into())
// Response: "An internal error occurred. Please try again later."
// Logs: Full error chain with connection details

items.title: Logged vs. Exposed
items.text: Error Type User Message Logged Details Internal "An internal error occurred" Full error chain, source file, line number ProcessingFailed "Processing failed. Check your request" Anyhow error with context chain ValidationFailed Specific validation message Validation report (non-sensitive) JobNotFound "Job not found: {id}" Job ID only

items.title: Core Error Handling
items.text:
• crates/service-http/src/v2/error.rs - Complete rewrite using thiserror
  • crates/service-http/Cargo.toml - Added thiserror = "2.0"

items.title: Handler Updates (simplified error handling)
items.text:
• crates/service-http/src/v2/handlers/common.rs
  • crates/service-http/src/v2/handlers/c2pa_video.rs
  • crates/service-http/src/v2/handlers/c2pa_fragmented.rs
  • crates/service-http/src/v2/handlers/c2pa_package.rs
  • crates/service-http/src/v2/handlers/package.rs

items.title: 1. Error Type Hierarchy
items.text:
• Use enum variants for different error categories
  • thiserror derives Error trait implementation
  • Each variant maps to appropriate HTTP status

items.title: 2. Source Error Tracking
items.text:
• #[source] attribute preserves error chains
  • Full context available for debugging
  • Supports error downcasting if needed

items.title: 3. Display vs. Debug
items.text:
• Display shows user-safe messages
  • Debug includes full error details for logging
  • IntoResponse uses Display for API responses

items.title: 4. Automatic Conversions
items.text:
• From implementations reduce boilerplate
  • Handlers use ? operator cleanly
  • Type inference handles conversions

items.title: 5. Contextual Error Creation
items.text:
• Constructor methods provide semantic clarity
  • validation_failed(), processing_failed(), etc.
  • Source errors attached where relevant

items.title: Unit Tests
items.text:
#[test]
fn test_error_messages_dont_leak_internals() {
    let internal_err = anyhow::anyhow!("Database password: secret123");
    let api_err = ApiError::internal(internal_err);

    let message = api_err.user_message();
    assert!(!message.contains("secret123"));
    assert_eq!(message, "An internal error occurred. Please try again later.");
}

items.title: Integration Tests
items.text:
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_invalid_input_returns_400() {
    let response = client
        .post("/v2/c2pa/video")
        .json(&invalid_request)
        .send()
        .await?;

    assert_eq!(response.status(), 400);

    let error: ApiErrorResponse = response.json().await?;
    assert_eq!(error.error.code, "VALIDATION_FAILED");
    assert!(!error.error.message.contains("internal"));
}

items.title: For New Endpoints
items.text:
1. Use ApiError as handler return type
  2. Leverage ? operator for error propagation
  3. Use semantic constructors: ApiError::validation_failed(), etc.
  4. Let From implementations handle conversions

items.title: For Existing Code
items.text:
1. Replace .map_err(|e| ApiError::internal_error(e.to_string())) with .map_err(ApiError::internal)
  2. Replace garde validation mapping with direct ?
  3. Remove explicit error string formatting for internal errors

items.title: Potential Additions
items.text:
1. Error IDs: Add unique error IDs for support ticket correlation
  2. Retry Hints: Include retry_after for transient errors
  3. Field-level Details: Expand ApiErrorDetails with field paths
  4. Localization: i18n support for error messages
  5. Metrics: Error rate tracking by error code

items.title: Extension Points
items.text:
// Add new error variants as needed
pub enum ApiError {
    // ... existing variants

    #[error("Rate limit exceeded")]
    RateLimitExceeded {
        retry_after: Duration,
    },

    #[error("Resource conflict")]
    Conflict {
        resource: String,
        #[source]
        source: Option<anyhow::Error>,
    },
}

items.title: References
items.text:
• Rust Error Handling Best Practices
  • thiserror Documentation
  • Axum Error Handling
  • Mode Instructions: rust-error-handler

items.title: Conclusion
items.text:
The refactored error handling system provides: - Type safety through Rust's type system and thiserror - Security by preventing information leakage - Observability through comprehensive error logging - Maintainability via reduced boilerplate and clear patterns - Consistency across all API endpoints

All handlers now follow a uniform error handling pattern that is both secure and developer-friendly.

items.title: Fix: Send Trait Issue in package_and_sign_processor
path: Fix: Send Trait Issue in package_and_sign_processor

items.title: Problem
items.text:
The async function sign_fragmented had a Send trait issue:

error: future cannot be sent between threads safely
   --> infra/src/media_packager/package_and_sign_processor.rs:218:5
    |
218 |     async fn process(
    |     ^^^^^ future created by async block is not `Send`
    |
    = help: the trait `Sync` is not implemented for `dyn Signer`
note: future is not `Send` as this value is used across an await
   --> infra/src/media_packager/package_and_sign_processor.rs:142:59
    |
 96 |         let signer = self.c2pa_context.signer()?;
    |             ------ has type `&dyn Signer` which is not `Send`
...
142 |             tokio::fs::create_dir_all(&signed_output_dir).await?;
    |                                                           ^^^^^ await occurs here, with `signer` maybe used later
path: Fix: Send Trait Issue in package_and_sign_processor

items.title: Root Cause
items.text:
The dyn Signer trait object does not implement Send or Sync, which is required for futures that can be sent between threads. The issue occurred because:

  1. signer was obtained early in the function
  2. An .await point occurred while signer was still in scope
  3. The compiler couldn't guarantee thread safety
path: Fix: Send Trait Issue in package_and_sign_processor

items.title: Solution
items.text: Restructured the sign_fragmented method to ensure signer is not held across any .await points:
path: Fix: Send Trait Issue in package_and_sign_processor

items.title: Before
items.text:
async fn sign_fragmented(...) {
    // Get builder and signer EARLY
    let mut builder = c2pa::Builder::from_shared_context(&self.c2pa_context);
    let signer = self.c2pa_context.signer()?;

    for init in inits {
        // ... collect data ...

        // AWAIT while signer is in scope ❌
        tokio::fs::create_dir_all(&signed_output_dir).await?;

        builder.sign_fragmented_files(signer, ...)?;
    }
}
path: Fix: Send Trait Issue in package_and_sign_processor

items.title: After
items.text:
async fn sign_fragmented(...) {
    // Collect all jobs WITHOUT getting signer
    let mut sign_jobs = Vec::new();

    for init in inits {
        // ... collect data into sign_jobs ...
        sign_jobs.push(SignJob { init_path, fragments, output_dir });
    }

    // Do ALL async operations BEFORE getting signer
    for job in &sign_jobs {
        tokio::fs::create_dir_all(&job.output_dir).await?;
    }

    // Get builder and signer AFTER all await points
    let mut builder = c2pa::Builder::from_shared_context(&self.c2pa_context);
    let signer = self.c2pa_context.signer()?;

    // Now do all sync signing operations (no await) ✅
    for job in sign_jobs {
        builder.sign_fragmented_files(signer, &job.init_path, &job.fragments, &job.output_dir)?;
    }
}
path: Fix: Send Trait Issue in package_and_sign_processor

items.title: Key Changes
items.text:
1. Phase 1: Collect - Gather all init segments and fragments into a Vec<SignJob>
  2. Phase 2: Prepare - Create all output directories (async, before getting signer)
  3. Phase 3: Sign - Get signer and sign all files (sync, no await points)

items.text: ✅ Thread Safety: Signer is never held across await points ✅ Performance: Creates directories in parallel before signing ✅ Correctness: No race conditions or Send trait violations ✅ Clarity: Clear separation of async and sync operations

2. Create all output directories (async, no signer)
   ↓
3. Get signer (after all awaits)
   ↓
4. Sign all files (sync, no more awaits)

items.text: Successfully refactored the service-http/src/v2/handlers.rs monolithic file into a well-organized module structure with the From trait pattern for type conversions.

items.text:
Split the single handlers.rs file into logical submodules:

crates/service-http/src/v2/
├── conversions.rs              # NEW: From/TryFrom implementations
├── handlers/                   # NEW: Module directory
│   ├── mod.rs                  # Re-exports
│   ├── common.rs               # Shared job submission logic
│   ├── c2pa_video.rs           # C2PA video signing handlers

│   └── status.rs               # Job status & health check

items.title: Before (Procedural)
items.text:
fn to_batch_spec(
    inputs: Vec<domain::InputRef>,
    processor_kind: &str,
    params: serde_json::Value,
    output: Option<OutputSpec>,
    options: ProcessingOptions,
) -> BatchSpec {
    BatchSpec {
        inputs,
        processor: ProcessorSpec {
            kind: processor_kind.to_string(),
            params,
        },
        output: output.map(|o| match o {
            OutputSpec::LocalPath { base } => DomainOutputSpec::LocalPath { base },
        }),
        options: BatchOptions {
            max_files_parallel: options.max_files_parallel,
        },
    }
}

impl From<ProcessingOptions> for BatchOptions { ... }

items.title: Using From Pattern in Handlers
items.text:
Before: 

pub async fn c2pa_video_async(...) -> ... {
    let params = serde_json::to_value(&req.params)
        .map_err(|e| ApiError::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;
    let spec = to_batch_spec(req.inputs, "c2pa_sign", params, req.output, req.options);
    submit_job_async(&state, spec).await
}

After: 

pub async fn c2pa_video_async(...) -> ... {
    let builder: BatchSpecBuilder = req.clone().try_into()
        .map_err(|e: serde_json::Error| ApiError::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;
    let spec = builder.build(req.inputs, req.output, req.options);
    submit_job_async(&state, spec).await
}

items.title: Builder Pattern
items.text:
// In conversions.rs
pub struct BatchSpecBuilder {
    pub processor_kind: String,
    pub params: serde_json::Value,
}

impl BatchSpecBuilder {
    pub fn build(
        self,
        inputs: Vec<domain::InputRef>,
        output: Option<OutputSpec>,
        options: ProcessingOptions,
    ) -> BatchSpec {
        BatchSpec {
            inputs,
            processor: ProcessorSpec {
                kind: self.processor_kind,
                params: self.params,
            },
            output: output.map(|o| o.into()),
            options: options.into(),
        }
    }
}

items.title: For New Handlers
items.text:
When adding a new handler:

  1. Create new file in handlers/ (e.g., image.rs)
  2. Implement handler functions
  3. Add mod image; to handlers/mod.rs
  4. Re-export handlers: pub use image::{...};
  5. Add conversion in conversions.rs if needed

Example:

    // Implementation
}

items.title: Overview
items.text:
Refactored the infra crate to use thiserror for structured, general error handling that provides clear error categories without exposing implementation details.

Date: March 9, 2026 Scope: crates/infra/src/error/ Architecture: Hexagonal (Ports & Adapters)

items.title: Goals Achieved
items.text:
1. ✅ Type-safe error handling using thiserror with #[from] attribute
  2. ✅ General error categories - no overly specific error variants
  3. ✅ Modular organization - separate files per concern for clarity
  4. ✅ Automatic error conversion - leveraging #[from] for common cases
  5. ✅ Clean error messages - simple, non-leaking error descriptions
  6. ✅ Backward compatibility - automatic conversion to anyhow::Error

items.title: Module Structure
items.text:
crates/infra/src/error/
├── mod.rs            # Top-level InfraError + exports
├── processor.rs      # Processor & C2PA errors
├── resolver.rs       # Input resolution errors
├── store.rs          # Job storage errors
└── sink.rs           # Output sink errors

items.title: Error Type Hierarchy
items.text:
pub enum InfraError {
    Processor { #[from] source: ProcessorError },
    Resolver { #[from] source: ResolverError },
    Store { #[from] source: StoreError },
    Sink { #[from] source: SinkError },
}

pub enum ProcessorError {
    C2paSigning { #[from] source: C2paError },
    MediaPackaging { #[from] source: MediaPackagerError },
    InvalidParams { message, source },
    Io { #[from] source: std::io::Error },
    UnsupportedFormat,
    Configuration { message },
}

pub enum C2paError {
    Sdk { #[from] source: c2pa::Error },
    ConfigFile { source: c2pa::Error },
    TaskExecution { #[from] source: tokio::task::JoinError },
}

pub enum ResolverError {
    UnsupportedInput,
    FeatureNotEnabled,
    PathNotFound,
    HttpRequest { #[from] source: reqwest::Error },
    InvalidUrl { #[from] source: url::ParseError },
    UnsupportedScheme,
    Io { #[from] source: std::io::Error },
    StreamConversion,
    InvalidContentType,
}

pub enum StoreError {
    JobNotFound,
    Io { #[from] source: std::io::Error },
    Serialization { #[from] source: serde_json::Error },
    DatabaseOperation,
    LockFailed,
}

pub enum SinkError {
    Io { #[from] source: std::io::Error },
    StreamProcessing,
    InvalidOutputSpec,
    CloudStorage,
}

items.title: 1. General Error Categories
items.text:
Errors are categorized by concern, not by specific failure modes:

// ✅ General
#[error("I/O operation failed")]
Io { #[from] source: std::io::Error }

// ❌ Too specific (avoided)
#[error("Failed to write file: {path}")]
FileWrite { path: String, source: std::io::Error }

items.title: 2. Automatic Conversion with #[from]
items.text:
Use #[from] attribute for automatic error conversion:

#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum ProcessorError {
    #[error("I/O operation failed")]
    Io {
        #[from]
        source: std::io::Error,
    },
}

// Usage - automatic conversion!
fn process_file() -> Result<(), ProcessorError> {
    let file = std::fs::File::open("test.txt")?;  // io::Error auto-converts
    Ok(())
}

items.title: 3. Modular Organization
items.text: Each error module is self-contained: - processor.rs - Processor and C2PA errors - resolver.rs - Input resolution errors - store.rs - Job storage errors - sink.rs - Output sink errors - mod.rs - Top-level type and exports

items.title: 4. No Redundant Constructors
items.text:
Errors use direct enum construction - no helper methods needed:

// ✅ Direct construction
return Err(ProcessorError::UnsupportedFormat);
return Err(ResolverError::PathNotFound);

// ❌ Redundant helpers (removed)
// ProcessorError::unsupported_format()
// ResolverError::path_not_found()

items.title: Processor Errors
items.text:
Purpose: Errors during processor execution (C2PA, media packaging)

ProcessorError::C2paSigning           // C2PA signing failed
ProcessorError::MediaPackaging        // Media packaging failed
ProcessorError::InvalidParams         // Invalid parameters
ProcessorError::Io                    // I/O operation failed
ProcessorError::UnsupportedFormat     // Unsupported format
ProcessorError::Configuration         // Configuration error

items.title: C2PA Errors
items.text:
Purpose: C2PA SDK-specific errors

C2paError::Sdk               // C2PA SDK error
C2paError::ConfigFile        // Configuration file error
C2paError::TaskExecution     // Async task failed

items.title: Resolver Errors
items.text:
Purpose: Input resolution failures

ResolverError::UnsupportedInput    // Input type not supported
ResolverError::FeatureNotEnabled   // Feature not enabled
ResolverError::PathNotFound        // Path not found
ResolverError::HttpRequest         // HTTP request failed
ResolverError::InvalidUrl          // Invalid URL
ResolverError::UnsupportedScheme   // Unsupported URL scheme
ResolverError::Io                  // I/O error
ResolverError::StreamConversion    // Stream conversion failed
ResolverError::InvalidContentType  // Invalid content type

items.title: Basic Error Handling
items.text:
use infra::ProcessorError;

fn process_media() -> Result<(), ProcessorError> {
    // I/O errors auto-convert via #[from]
    let data = std::fs::read("input.mp4")?;

    if data.is_empty() {
        return Err(ProcessorError::UnsupportedFormat);
    }

    Ok(())
}

items.title: Error Conversion Chain
items.text:
// c2pa::Error -> C2paError -> ProcessorError -> InfraError
fn sign_media() -> Result<(), InfraError> {
    let signer = get_signer()?;  // c2pa::Error
    signer.sign()?;               // Automatically converted through chain
    Ok(())
}

items.title: Pattern Matching
items.text:
match result {
    Ok(artifact) => process_artifact(artifact),
    Err(ProcessorError::C2paSigning { source }) => {
        error!("C2PA signing failed: {}", source);
    }
    Err(ProcessorError::InvalidParams { message, .. }) => {
        warn!("Invalid params: {}", message);
    }
    Err(e) => {
        error!("Processing failed: {}", e);
    }
}

items.title: 1. Use #[from] for Common Conversions
items.text:
#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum ProcessorError {
    #[error("I/O operation failed")]
    Io {
        #[from]  // Automatic conversion from io::Error
        source: std::io::Error,
    },
}

items.title: 2. Keep Error Messages General
items.text:
// ✅ Good - general
#[error("HTTP request failed")]
HttpRequest { source: reqwest::Error }

// ❌ Too specific - avoid
#[error("HTTP request to {url} failed with status {status}")]
HttpRequestFailed { url: String, status: u16, source: reqwest::Error }

items.title: 3. Use Direct Construction
items.text:
// ✅ Good - direct
return Err(ResolverError::PathNotFound);

// ❌ Redundant - avoid
return Err(ResolverError::path_not_found());

items.title: 4. Preserve Error Context
items.text:
#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum ProcessorError {
    #[error("Invalid processor parameters: {message}")]
    InvalidParams {
        message: String,
        #[source]  // Preserves underlying error
        source: Option<serde_json::Error>,
    },
}

• Use ProcessorError, ResolverError, etc. directly
  • Leverage ? operator for automatic conversion
  • Use #[from] for common error types

items.title: For Existing Code
items.text:

  • No breaking changes for domain/app layers
  • Gradual migration possible

items.title: Testing Recommendations
items.text:
#[test]
fn test_error_conversion() {
    let io_err = std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound, "test");
    let proc_err: ProcessorError = io_err.into();
    assert!(matches!(proc_err, ProcessorError::Io { .. }));
}

#[test]
fn test_error_chain() {
    let c2pa_err = C2paError::Sdk { source: c2pa::Error::BadParam("test".into()) };
    let proc_err: ProcessorError = c2pa_err.into();
    assert!(matches!(proc_err, ProcessorError::C2paSigning { .. }));
}

#[test]
fn test_anyhow_conversion() {
    let proc_err = ProcessorError::UnsupportedFormat;
    let anyhow_err: anyhow::Error = proc_err.into();
    assert_eq!(anyhow_err.to_string(), "Unsupported media format");
}

impl ProcessorError {

        match self {

items.title: Overview
items.text:
The package_and_sign processor is a combined processor that:

  1. Packages multiple resolution MP4 files into DASH/HLS format using FFmpeg and Bento4
  2. Signs the packaged fragmented MP4 content with C2PA metadata in place

This processor eliminates the need to run packaging and signing as separate steps.

items.title: Processor Kind
items.text: package_and_sign

items.title: Input Type
items.text: • local_folder_path: A folder containing multiple MP4 files (different resolutions)

items.title: Parameters
items.text: Parameter Type Required Default Description output_name string Yes - Name for the output packaged content playlist_pattern string No **/playlist.m3u8 Glob pattern to match playlist files init_pattern string No **/init_*.mp4 Glob pattern to match init segments frag_pattern string No seg_*.m4s Glob pattern to match fragment segments

items.title: Step 1: Packaging
items.text:
1. Scans the input folder for MP4 files
  2. Demuxes video and audio streams in parallel
  3. Fragments each stream with Bento4 (mp4fragment)
  4. Packages all streams into DASH/HLS format with Bento4 (mp4dash)

items.title: Step 2: C2PA Signing (In Place)
items.text:
1. Scans the packaged output for init segments matching init_pattern
  2. For each init segment, finds corresponding fragments matching frag_pattern
  3. Signs the init segment and fragments with C2PA metadata
  4. Writes signed content in place (no separate signed folder)

items.title: Configuration
items.text:
The processor uses two configuration files:

  1. Pipeline Config: configs/media_packager_pipeline.toml - Controls demux, fragment, and package settings
  2. C2PA Settings: configs/default_c2pa_sdk_settings.toml - Controls C2PA signing behavior

items.title: Overview
items.text: Refactored FragmentedSigner::sign_all() to sign multiple init segments in parallel instead of sequentially, significantly improving performance for multi-track content.

items.title: Before (Sequential Signing)
items.text:
// Sign one init segment at a time
for job in jobs {
    builder.sign_fragmented_files(signer, &job.init_path, &job.fragments, &job.output_dir)?;
    signed_count += 1;
}

Performance: If you have 6 tracks (4 video + 2 audio), and each takes 10 seconds to sign: - Total time: 60 seconds (sequential)

items.title: After (Parallel Signing)
items.text:
// Sign all init segments concurrently using tokio tasks
let sign_tasks = jobs.into_iter().map(|job| {
    tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
        // Each task gets its own builder and signer
        let mut builder = c2pa::Builder::from_shared_context(&context);
        builder.sign_fragmented_files(signer, ...)?;
    })
}).collect();

futures::future::join_all(sign_tasks).await;

Performance: Same 6 tracks, each taking 10 seconds: - Total time: ~10 seconds (parallel, limited by slowest track) - Speedup: ~6x faster 🚀

items.title: Phase 1: Create Directories (Parallel)
items.text:
// Create all output directories in parallel
let dir_tasks = jobs.iter().map(|job| {
    tokio::fs::create_dir_all(job.output_dir.clone())
});
futures::future::try_join_all(dir_tasks).await?;

items.title: Phase 2: Sign in Parallel (Blocking Tasks)
items.text:
for job in jobs {
    let sign_task = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
        // Each task gets its own builder and signer
        let mut builder = c2pa::Builder::from_shared_context(&context);
        builder.set_intent(intent);
        let signer = context.signer()?;

        // Sign this init segment + fragments
        builder.sign_fragmented_files(signer, &job.init_path, &job.fragments, &job.output_dir)?;
    });
    sign_tasks.push(sign_task);
}

// Wait for all to complete
let results = futures::future::join_all(sign_tasks).await;

items.title: 1. Why spawn_blocking?
items.text: C2PA signing operations are CPU-intensive and synchronous. Using spawn_blocking: - ✅ Moves blocking work off the async runtime - ✅ Allows true parallelism across CPU cores - ✅ Prevents blocking the tokio runtime

items.title: 2. Why Clone Context?
items.text: Each task needs its own copy of the context: - ✅ Thread-safe: Arc<c2pa::Context> is cheaply cloneable - ✅ Independent signers: Each task gets its own signer instance - ✅ No shared mutable state

items.title: 3. Why Clone Intent?
items.text: c2pa::BuilderIntent is cloneable and needs to be sent to each task: - ✅ Each builder needs the same intent - ✅ Cheap to clone (small enum)

items.title: 4. Error Handling
items.text:
let results = futures::future::join_all(sign_tasks).await;

for result in results {
    match result {
        Ok(Ok(())) => signed_count += 1,
        Ok(Err(e)) => return Err(e),  // Signing error
        Err(e) => return Err(...),     // Task panic
    }
}
- ✅ Waits for all tasks to complete - ✅ Returns immediately on first error - ✅ Handles both signing errors and task panics

items.title: Send-Safe Implementation ✅
items.text:
The implementation is Send-safe because:

  1. No shared mutable state - Each task is independent
  2. Arc - Thread-safe, cheaply cloneable
  3. spawn_blocking - Moves blocking work off async runtime
  4. Each task gets its own signer - No shared signer across await points

items.title: Memory Safety ✅
items.text:
• Each FragmentedSignJob is moved into its own task
  • No data races or concurrent access issues
  • Clean task boundaries

items.title: Impact on Processors
items.text: All three processors automatically benefit from parallel signing:

items.title: 1. PackageAndSignProcessor
items.text:
self.signer.sign_fragmented(...).await?;  // Now signs in parallel!
Benefit: Faster packaging + signing workflow

items.title: 2. C2pafMP4Processor
items.text:
self.signer.sign_fragmented(...).await?;  // Now signs in parallel!
Benefit: Faster HLS publication signing

items.title: 3. Direct Usage
items.text:
let signer = FragmentedSigner::from_sdk_toml("config.toml")?;
signer.sign_fragmented(...).await?;  // Parallel by default!

items.title: Backwards Compatibility
items.text: ✅ API unchanged - Same public interface ✅ Behavior unchanged - Same output, just faster ✅ Configuration unchanged - No new settings needed ✅ Zero breaking changes

items.title: CPU
items.text:
• Before: ~16% (1 core)
  • After: Up to 100% across available cores
  • Recommendation: Works best on multi-core systems

items.title: Memory
items.text:
• Minimal increase: Each task duplicates builder/signer (small overhead)
  • Typical increase: < 10MB total for 10 tracks
  • No memory leaks: Tasks clean up after completion

items.title: I/O
items.text:
• Parallel directory creation: Faster
  • Parallel file writes: May saturate disk I/O on slow disks
  • Recommendation: Works best with SSD

items.title: What Happens If One Task Fails?
items.text:
// Task 1: ✅ Success
// Task 2: ❌ Error (corrupted init segment)
// Task 3: ⏳ Still running
// Task 4: ⏳ Still running

Behavior: 1. All tasks continue to completion 2. First error is detected 3. Error is returned immediately 4. Other tasks complete (but results ignored)

Why not cancel remaining tasks? - Simpler error handling - Minimal wasted work (signing is fast) - Consistent behavior

items.title: How to Verify Parallel Signing
items.text:
1. Check logs - Should see multiple "Signing init segment" messages at once
  2. Monitor CPU - Should see higher CPU usage (multiple cores)
  3. Time the operation - Should be faster with multiple tracks
  4. Check output - All files should be signed correctly

items.title: Send-Safe Signing
items.text:
The FragmentedSigner::sign_all() method uses a three-phase approach to avoid holding dyn Signer across await points:

  1. Phase 1: Collect - Gather all signing jobs (no signer)
  2. Phase 2: Prepare - Create directories async (no signer)
  3. Phase 3: Sign - Get signer and sign sync (no awaits)

This eliminates Send trait violations while maintaining clean async code.

items.title: Changes Made
path: Package and Sign Processor - Refactoring Summary

items.title: Overview
items.text: Refactored the package_and_sign processor to use a temporary directory for the packaging phase, then copy signed segments and manifests to the final output directory.
path: Package and Sign Processor - Refactoring Summary

items.title: Key Changes
path: Package and Sign Processor - Refactoring Summary

items.title: 1. Temporary Directory for Packaging
items.text:
• Before: Packaged directly to final output directory
  • After: Uses tempfile crate to create a temporary directory for packaging
  • Benefit: Clean separation of packaging and signing phases, automatic cleanup
path: Package and Sign Processor - Refactoring Summary

items.title: 2. Sign from Source to Destination
items.text:
• Before: Attempted to sign in place (which is not supported by builder.sign_fragmented_files)
  • After: Signs from temporary packaging directory to final output directory
  • Method: sign_fragmented() now takes source and destination directories
  • Benefit: Properly uses the C2PA API as intended
path: Package and Sign Processor - Refactoring Summary

items.title: 3. Copy All Manifests and Playlists
items.text:
• New Feature: copy_manifests() method
  • Patterns: Copies all *.m3u8 and *.mpd files recursively
  • Directory Structure: Maintains relative paths from source to destination
  • Benefit: Ensures all playlists and manifests are included in final output
path: Package and Sign Processor - Refactoring Summary

items.title: Workflow
items.text:
Input MP4 files
    ↓
1. Package to TEMP directory
    ├── Demux (parallel)
    ├── Fragment (parallel)
    └── Package (DASH/HLS)
    ↓
2. Sign TEMP → FINAL
    ├── Find init segments in TEMP
    ├── Find fragments for each init
    ├── Sign to FINAL directory
    └── Maintain directory structure
    ↓
3. Copy manifests TEMP → FINAL
    ├── Copy all *.m3u8 files
    ├── Copy all *.mpd files
    └── Maintain directory structure
    ↓
4. Cleanup TEMP directory
    └── Automatically deleted when dropped
    ↓
Final signed output ready
path: Package and Sign Processor - Refactoring Summary

pub finished_at_ms: Option<u64>,

// After:
pub started_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
pub finished_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,

Benefits: - Native datetime handling - Automatic ISO 8601 serialization - Type-safe date operations - No manual timestamp conversion needed

items.title: Code Quality Improvements
path: V1 API Deprecation and Conversions Removal - Summary

items.title: Before (Conversions Layer)
items.text:
// conversions.rs
fn millis_to_datetime(millis: u64) -> DateTime<Utc> {
    let secs = (millis / 1000) as i64;
    let nanos = ((millis % 1000) * 1_000_000) as u32;
    Utc.timestamp_opt(secs, nanos).unwrap()
}

pub fn snapshot_to_result(snapshot: BatchSnapshot) -> JobResultResponse {
    let queued_at = if let Some(started_ms) = snapshot.started_at_ms {
        millis_to_datetime(started_ms.saturating_sub(1000))
    } else {
        Utc::now()
    };
    // ... more conversion logic
}
path: V1 API Deprecation and Conversions Removal - Summary

items.title: After (Direct Conversion)
items.text:
// dto.rs
impl From<BatchSnapshot> for JobResultResponse {
    fn from(snapshot: BatchSnapshot) -> Self {
        let queued_at = snapshot
            .started_at
            .map(|dt| dt - chrono::Duration::seconds(1))
            .unwrap_or_else(Utc::now);
        // ... clean, type-safe conversion
    }
}

items.title: API v2 Refactoring - Implementation Summary
items.text: Date: March 4, 2026 Status: ✅ Complete and Tested

items.title: Overview
items.text: Successfully refactored the HTTP API from a batch-centric design to a processor-centric design with feature-gated versioning. The new v2 API provides cleaner endpoints, better validation, and improved developer experience while maintaining backwards compatibility through feature flags.

items.title: 1. Feature-Gated API Versions
items.text:
• Added v1_api and v2_api feature flags to service-http/Cargo.toml
  • Default feature is v2_api
  • Compilation enforces at least one API version is enabled
  • Clean separation allows independent evolution of API versions

items.title: 2. New URL Structure (v2)
items.text:
Old (v1): - POST /v1/batches - Submit batch job - POST /v1/batches/sync - Submit and wait - GET /v1/batches/{id} - Get batch status

New (v2): - POST /v2/c2pa/video - Sign video files - POST /v2/c2pa/fragmented - Sign HLS content - POST /v2/package - Package media - POST /v2/c2pa/package - Package and sign - POST /v2/sync/... - Sync versions of all endpoints - GET /v2/jobs/{id} - Get job status - GET /v2/health - Health check

items.title: 3. Simplified Request Format
items.text:
Old (v1): 

{
  "inputs": [...],
  "processor": {
    "kind": "c2pa_sign",
    "params": {...}
  },
  "output": {...}
}

New (v2): 

{
  "inputs": [...],
  "params": {...},
  "output": {...},
  "options": {...}
}

Processor type is implicit from the endpoint URL, not specified in the request body.

items.title: 4. Enhanced Response Format
items.text: v2 Response includes: - job_id - Unique identifier - status - "queued", "running", "succeeded", "failed" - results[] - Per-input results with status and error messages - timing - ISO 8601 timestamps (queued_at, started_at, finished_at)

items.title: 5. Input Validation Per Endpoint
items.text:
Each endpoint validates that inputs match processor requirements:

  • /c2pa/video - Accepts file inputs only (rejects folders)
  • /c2pa/fragmented - Accepts HLS publication or folder inputs
  • /package - Accepts folder inputs containing MP4 files
  • /c2pa/package - Accepts folder inputs for packaging + signing

items.text:
service-http/src/
├── main.rs                    # Feature-gated router initialization
├── middleware.rs              # Shared middleware
├── multi_form/                # Multipart form handling
├── processing.rs              # Processor registry builder

│   ├── mod.rs

│   ├── handlers.rs            # V1 handlers
│   └── routes.rs              # V1 routes

    ├── mod.rs
