    ├── dto.rs                 # V2 request/response DTOs
    ├── error.rs               # Structured API errors
    ├── handlers.rs            # V2 endpoint handlers
    ├── routes.rs              # V2 routes
    └── validation.rs          # Input validators

items.title: 1. Reused Existing JobStore
items.text:
• Did not create duplicate job storage
  • Uses domain::JobStore trait already implemented in infra::InMemoryJobStore
  • Extensible to persistent storage (database) in the future

items.title: 2. Domain Type Conversions
items.text:
• Created conversions.rs module to map BatchSnapshot to v2 DTOs
  • Handles timestamp conversion (ms → ISO 8601)
  • Maps FileResult fields correctly (message → error, output.location → output_path)

items.title: 3. Input Validation
items.text:
• Created validator traits for each processor type
  • Compile-time safety through Rust's type system
  • Runtime validation with helpful error messages

items.title: 4. Error Handling
items.text:
• Unified ApiError type with error codes
  • Standardized error response format
  • Includes details for debugging (input_index, field, etc.)

items.title: DTOs Created
items.text:
Request DTOs: - C2paVideoRequest / C2paVideoParams - C2paFragmentedRequest / C2paFragmentedParams - PackageRequest / PackageParams - PackageAndSignRequest / PackageAndSignParams - ProcessingOptions (shared) - OutputSpec (shared)

Response DTOs: - JobResponse - Async submission response - JobResultResponse - Job completion result - ProcessingResult - Per-input result - JobTiming - Timestamp information

items.title: Handlers Implemented
items.text:
Each processor has two handlers: 1. *_async - Returns job_id immediately 2. *_sync - Blocks until completion or timeout

Implemented handlers: - c2pa_video_async / c2pa_video_sync - c2pa_fragmented_async / c2pa_fragmented_sync - package_async / package_sync - package_and_sign_async / package_and_sign_sync - get_job_status - health

items.title: Validation
items.text: Created three validators: - VideoInputValidator - Validates video file inputs - FragmentedInputValidator - Validates HLS/folder inputs - PackageInputValidator - Validates folder inputs

  • SYNC_POLL_MS - Polling interval (default: 250 ms)
  • RUST_LOG - Log level configuration

items.title: For API Consumers
items.text:
Old v1 request: 

curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/v1/batches \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "inputs": [{"type": "local_file_path", "path": "/video.mp4"}],
    "processor": {"kind": "c2pa_sign", "params": {}},
    "output": {"type": "local_path", "base": "/output"}
  }'

New v2 request: 

curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/v2/c2pa/video \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "inputs": [{"type": "local_file_path", "path": "/video.mp4"}],
    "params": {},
    "output": {"type": "local_path", "base": "/output"}
  }'

Key differences: 1. URL changed from /v1/batches to /v2/c2pa/video 2. Removed processor wrapper - processor type is in URL 3. params is top-level instead of nested 4. Added /v2/sync/... variants for synchronous execution

items.title: Lessons Learned
items.text:
1. Domain Reuse: Leveraging existing JobStore trait saved time and ensured consistency
  2. Feature Flags: Rust's feature system provides clean API versioning
  3. Type Safety: Input validation at the handler level prevents processor misuse
  4. Conversions: Separate conversion layer keeps domain and API concerns separate

items.title: Conclusion
items.text:
The v2 API refactoring successfully achieves:

✅ Processor-centric URL structure ✅ Simplified request format ✅ Enhanced response format with detailed results ✅ Input validation per endpoint ✅ Sync and async operation modes ✅ Feature-gated versioning ✅ Backwards compatibility through v1 API ✅ Extensible architecture ✅ Clean separation of concerns

The implementation is production-ready and provides a solid foundation for future enhancements.

items.title: Current Layout
items.text:
crates/service-http/src/v2/handlers/ is organized by public API capability:

  • status.rs for GET /v2/health and GET /v2/jobs/{id}
  • c2pa_video.rs for monolithic video signing
  • c2pa_fragmented.rs for fragmented/HLS signing
  • package.rs for packaging only
  • c2pa_package.rs for package-and-sign flows
  • softbinding.rs for placeholder watermark endpoints
  • common.rs for shared async/sync submission helpers

items.title: Request Handling Pattern
items.text:
Each processing handler follows the same shape:

  1. Deserialize a request DTO from crate::v2::dto::*
  2. Validate the DTO with garde
  3. Validate processor/input compatibility with crate::v2::validation::*InputValidator
  4. Convert the DTO into domain::BatchSpec
  5. Call submit_job_async or submit_job_sync

items.title: Response Conventions
items.text:
• Async processing endpoints return Json<JobResponse> with job_id and status
  • Sync processing endpoints return (StatusCode::OK, Json<JobResultResponse>)
  • Timeouts are surfaced as ApiError::timeout() and become HTTP 408
  • Placeholder watermark endpoints return HTTP 501 with body Coming soon

items.title: State And Shared Constraints
items.text:
Handlers receive AppState from crates/service-http/src/v2/routes.rs:

  • service: Arc<dyn BatchService>
  • sync_timeout_ms
  • sync_poll_interval_ms
  • allowed_s3_output_buckets

submit_job_async and submit_job_sync enforce:

  • BatchSpec::validate_spec()
  • S3 output validation against the configured allow-list

items.title: Adding A New Processing Endpoint
items.text:
1. Add the request DTO under crates/service-http/src/v2/dto/
  2. Add or reuse an input validator in crates/service-http/src/v2/validation.rs
  3. Implement async and sync handlers under crates/service-http/src/v2/handlers/
  4. Register both routes in crates/service-http/src/v2/routes.rs
  5. If the endpoint is public, add utoipa::path annotations so the OpenAPI doc stays complete
  6. Extend crates/service-http/src/v2/docs.rs tests if the public route set changes

items.title: Testing
items.text:
• Handler unit tests live beside the handlers
  • Router coverage lives in crates/service-http/src/v2/routes.rs
  • Integration behavior is exercised in worker_integration.rs

items.title: Overview
items.text: The c2pa-claim-generator project follows a comprehensive testing strategy with multiple levels of testing to ensure reliability, correctness, and performance.

items.title: 1. Unit Tests
items.text:
Location: Within each crate using #[cfg(test)] modules

Purpose: Test individual functions, methods, and components in isolation

Coverage: - Domain logic validation - Processor implementations - Input resolution logic - Configuration parsing - Error handling paths - Remote URL dispatch and S3 URL parsing

Example: 

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_batch_spec_validation() {
        // Test validation logic
    }
}

Running: 

cargo test --lib

items.title: 2. Integration Tests
items.text:
Location: tests/integration/

Purpose: Test interactions between crates and components

Coverage: - Cross-crate interactions - Processor registry - Job lifecycle management - Input resolver composition - Output sink operations - S3 startup validation and object staging when the s3 feature is enabled

Structure: 

tests/integration/
├── c2pa_signing_test.rs        # C2PA signing integration
├── media_packaging_test.rs     # Media packaging integration

Location: tests/e2e/

Purpose: Test complete workflows from HTTP request to output

Coverage: - Full HTTP API workflows - Async and sync endpoints - Real media file processing - Error scenarios - Concurrent job processing

Example workflow: 1. Start server 2. Submit job via HTTP 3. Poll for completion 4. Verify output files 5. Validate C2PA claims

Running:

items.title: Fixture Guidelines
items.text:
1. Minimal Size: Keep test files as small as possible
  2. Representative: Cover common formats and edge cases
  3. Documented: Include README in each fixture directory explaining files
  4. Reusable: Shared across unit, integration, and e2e tests
  5. Version Controlled: Check into Git (use LFS if needed)

items.title: CI Validation Gate
items.text:
The default CI gate is intentionally narrow and should stay fast and stable:

cargo fmt --all --check
cargo clippy-default
cargo test --workspace --no-run

That gate is the required baseline for normal pull requests.

items.title: Follow-up Validation Backlog
items.text:
Add these only after the default gate is stable:

  • cargo clippy-all
  • selected feature-matrix checks, especially infra resolver combinations and service-http --features s3
  • service-http docs feature checks:
  • cargo check -p service-http --features openapi
  • cargo check -p service-http --features "openapi scalar-docs"
  • cargo test -p service-http --features openapi
  • cargo test -p service-http --features "openapi scalar-docs"
  • full cargo test --workspace
  • stable line/region/function coverage jobs

Do not add branch coverage to CI until the LLVM crash in the branch reporting path is resolved.

items.title: 1. Test Isolation
items.text:
• Each test should be independent
  • Clean up artifacts after tests
  • Use temporary directories for output
  • Mock external dependencies

items.title: 2. Test Naming
items.text:
#[test]
fn test_<component>_<scenario>_<expected_outcome>() {
    // Example: test_c2pa_processor_invalid_input_returns_error
}

items.title: 3. Assertions
items.text:
• Use descriptive assertion messages
  • Test both success and failure paths
  • Validate side effects (files created, logs written)
  • Prefer assert_matches! for enum/result/option variants when the type is the contract under test
  • Assert on error variants/types rather than error strings
  • Only assert on error messages when the message itself is a user-facing or API-facing contract
use std::assert_matches::assert_matches;

assert_matches!(result, Ok(_));
assert_matches!(err, DomainError::InputResolution);

items.title: 3a. Test Refactor Step
items.text:
As part of ongoing test maintenance, add a refactor pass that updates older tests to follow the same pattern:

  • replace string-based error assertions with type/variant assertions where the error type is the real contract
  • replace assert!(matches!(...)) with assert_matches! unless plain boolean assertions are materially clearer

items.title: 4. Async Tests
items.text:
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_async_operation() {
    let result = async_function().await;
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}

items.title: 5. Parallel Execution
items.text:
• Tests run in parallel by default
  • Use #[serial] attribute for tests requiring serialization
  • Avoid shared mutable state

items.title: 6. Test Coverage
items.text:
# Install coverage tool
cargo install cargo-llvm-cov

# Summary report
cargo llvm-cov --workspace --summary-only

# HTML report
cargo llvm-cov --workspace --html

# Summary report with branch coverage
./scripts/dev/cov-branches.sh

Branch coverage is currently an unstable cargo-llvm-cov feature. Use the repo script instead of raw cargo llvm-cov --branch so coverage export uses the LLVM tools bundled with the active Rust toolchain rather than the system LLVM.

items.title: MockJobStore
items.text:
struct MockJobStore {
    jobs: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<JobId, Job>>>,
}

impl JobStore for MockJobStore {
    // Implementation for testing
}

items.text:
struct MockInputResolver {
    responses: HashMap<InputRef, ResolvedInput>,
}

impl InputResolver for MockInputResolver {
    // Return pre-configured responses
}

items.title: MockOutputSink
items.text:
struct MockOutputSink {
    captured_artifacts: Arc<Mutex<Vec<ProcessedArtifact>>>,
}

impl OutputSink for MockOutputSink {
    // Capture artifacts for verification
}

name: Tests

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3

  • App crate: 80%+ coverage (orchestration)

items.title: Purpose
items.text: Use this flow when you want runtime API docs endpoints or need to regenerate the raw OpenAPI file consumed by the docs site.

items.title: Runtime OpenAPI Endpoints
items.text:
Enable the runtime raw spec:

cargo run -p service-http --features openapi

Runtime endpoint:

  • /openapi.json

Enable Scalar as well:

cargo run -p service-http --features "openapi scalar-docs"

Additional runtime endpoint:

  • /docs

items.title: Docs-Site Raw Spec
items.text:
The docs site uses a generated raw spec at docs/api/openapi.json.

Generate it without starting the HTTP server:

make docs-openapi

The generation path uses the full documentation feature profile:

  • openapi
  • s3

items.title: Build The Full Docs Site
items.text: make docs

items.title: Preview The Full Docs Site

items.title: Swagger Editor Card
items.text:
The docs homepage includes a Swagger Editor card that can preload api/openapi.json when the docs site is hosted at a public URL.

When you browse the docs locally, the card still opens Swagger Editor, but without preloading the spec. In local workflows, use one of these instead:

  • the generated docs asset at api/openapi.json
  • the runtime /docs endpoint when service-http is built with --features "openapi scalar-docs"

items.title: Export The Product Definition Pack
items.text:
Create the curated source-doc ZIP for product, executive, and partner review:

make docs-export-product

Generated artifact:

  • dist/c2pa-claim-generator-product-definition-docs.zip

items.text:

  • Documentation Index

items.title: Configuration Guide
items.text: This service uses a typed top-level configuration file plus environment overrides.

items.title: Overview
items.text:
Configuration is split into two layers:

  • config/service.toml
  • service bootstrap settings
  • server parameters
  • worker parameters
  • input resolver settings
  • output sink settings
  • paths to processor-specific TOML files
  • specialized processor TOMLs
  • config/c2pa/*.toml
  • config/media-packager/*.toml

The service loads config/service.toml by default. Set SERVICE_CONFIG_PATH to point at a different file.

items.title: Service Config
items.text:
Example:

[server]
bind_addr = "0.0.0.0:8080"
max_upload_bytes = 52428800
sync_timeout_ms = 120000
sync_poll_interval_ms = 250

[worker]
queue_capacity = 128
max_concurrent_batches = 4
default_max_files_parallel = 4

[input_resolvers.s3]
# profile = "my-aws-profile"
force_path_style = false
buckets = []

[output_sinks.local]
base_dir = "./.artifacts/output"

[output_sinks.s3]
# region = "eu-central-1"
# force_path_style = false
# buckets = ["processed-media", "archive-media"]
# base_prefix = "exports"

[processors.c2pa]
sdk_settings_path = "config/c2pa/default_c2pa_sdk_settings.toml"

[processors.media_packager]
pipeline_config_path = "config/media-packager/media_packager_pipeline.toml"

items.title: server
items.text:
• bind_addr: address and port for the HTTP server
  • max_upload_bytes: maximum HTTP request body size accepted by Axum
  • sync_timeout_ms: sync endpoint timeout
  • sync_poll_interval_ms: sync endpoint polling interval

items.title: worker
items.text:
• queue_capacity: job queue size
  • max_concurrent_batches: maximum batches processed in parallel
  • default_max_files_parallel: default per-batch input parallelism

items.title: input_resolvers.s3
items.text:
• presence of this section enables S3 resolver bootstrap
  • profile: optional AWS shared config profile; when omitted the default credential chain is used
  • region: optional AWS region
  • endpoint_url: optional custom endpoint for MinIO/LocalStack
  • force_path_style: enable path-style addressing
  • buckets: allowed buckets and startup validation targets

items.title: output_sinks.local
items.text: • base_dir: base directory for persisted outputs

items.title: output_sinks.s3
items.text:
• optional output sink configuration under the existing s3 feature
  • profile: optional AWS shared config profile
  • region: optional AWS region
  • endpoint_url: optional custom S3-compatible endpoint
  • force_path_style: enable path-style addressing
  • buckets: writable bucket allow-list for request-selected S3 outputs
  • base_prefix: optional shared prefix prepended to every uploaded object key

Behavior:

  • there is no default S3 output bucket
  • request payloads must explicitly choose output.type = "s3" with bucket and prefix
  • only configured buckets are accepted
  • configured output buckets are write-probed during startup
  • prefix = "/" writes directly under the configured base_prefix root; if no base_prefix is configured, objects are written at the bucket root under the batch prefix

items.title: processors
items.text:
These sections reference specialized processor configuration files instead of inlining their internals:

  • processors.c2pa.sdk_settings_path
  • processors.media_packager.pipeline_config_path

items.title: Environment Overrides
items.text:
Environment variables override service.toml values using the SERVICE__... prefix.

Examples:

export SERVICE_CONFIG_PATH=config/service.prod.toml
export SERVICE__SERVER__BIND_ADDR=0.0.0.0:9090
export SERVICE__SERVER__SYNC_TIMEOUT_MS=90000
export SERVICE__WORKER__QUEUE_CAPACITY=256
export SERVICE__INPUT_RESOLVERS__S3__PROFILE=my-aws-profile
export SERVICE__INPUT_RESOLVERS__S3__REGION=eu-central-1
export SERVICE__INPUT_RESOLVERS__S3__BUCKETS=media-a,media-b
export SERVICE__OUTPUT_SINKS__S3__REGION=eu-central-1
export SERVICE__OUTPUT_SINKS__S3__BUCKETS=processed-media,archive-media
export SERVICE__OUTPUT_SINKS__S3__BASE_PREFIX=exports

Use environment variables for:

  • environment-specific operational values
  • deployment overrides
  • secret references and cloud runtime integration

Keep structured defaults in service.toml.

items.title: Container and Cloud Usage
items.text:
Recommended pattern:

  • mount config/service.toml from a ConfigMap or baked-in image config
  • mount processor-specific TOMLs separately if needed
  • use SERVICE__... for deployment-specific overrides
  • use the AWS default credential chain for S3 access instead of storing credentials in TOML

This works well for:

  • Docker
  • Kubernetes
  • GitHub Actions
  • AWS-hosted deployments

items.title: Feature Flags
items.text:
• openapi: exposes /openapi.json
  • scalar-docs: extends openapi and exposes /docs
  • s3: enables S3-backed input resolvers and request-selectable S3 outputs

The docs build generates a raw OpenAPI file through make docs-openapi using the documentation feature profile, which enables both openapi and s3.

If [input_resolvers.s3] or [output_sinks.s3] is configured without building the service with --features s3, startup fails.

items.title: Request Input Types
items.text:
The current request model is provider-agnostic:

  • local_file_path
  • local_folder_path
  • remote_file_url
  • remote_folder_url
  • in_request

Examples:

{"type": "remote_file_url", "url": "https://example.com/video.mp4"}
{"type": "remote_file_url", "url": "s3://media-bucket/input/video.mp4"}
{"type": "remote_folder_url", "url": "s3://media-bucket/publication/42/"}

items.title: Request Output Types
items.text:
Current supported output types:

  • configured local output by omitting the output field
  • s3 when the service is built with the s3 feature and output buckets are configured

Examples:

null
{"type": "s3", "bucket": "processed-media", "prefix": "c2pa/video"}
{"type": "s3", "bucket": "processed-media", "prefix": "/"}

items.title: Prerequisites
items.text:
• Linux or macOS
  • Rust toolchain with edition 2024 support
  • FFmpeg available on PATH
  • Bento4 tools available on PATH, especially mp4fragment and mp4dash

Verify the external tools:

ffmpeg -version
mp4fragment --version
mp4dash --version

items.title: Build The Workspace
items.text:
cargo build --workspace

Optional validation before first run:

cargo test --workspace --no-run

items.title: Configure The Service
items.text:
The HTTP service loads config/service.toml by default. The checked-in default enables:

  • bind on 0.0.0.0:8080
  • local output sink at ./.artifacts/output
  • C2PA settings from config/c2pa/default_c2pa_sdk_settings.toml
  • media packager pipeline from config/media-packager/media_packager_pipeline.toml

Useful overrides:

export RUST_LOG=info
export SERVICE_CONFIG_PATH=config/service.toml
export SERVICE__SERVER__BIND_ADDR=127.0.0.1:8080
export SERVICE__SERVER__SYNC_TIMEOUT_MS=120000

See the Configuration Guide for the full runtime model, including S3 and feature-gated behavior.

items.title: Start The Service
items.text:
cargo run -p service-http

To expose generated API docs:

cargo run -p service-http --features openapi
cargo run -p service-http --features "openapi scalar-docs"

To preview the docs site itself:

make docs-serve

items.title: Health Probe
items.text:
curl -i http://localhost:8080/v2/health

Expected result: HTTP 200 OK with an empty body.

items.title: Sign A Video Synchronously
items.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/v2/sync/c2pa/video \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "inputs": [
      {
        "type": "local_file_path",
        "path": "./tests/fixtures/video/mp4/video1.mp4"
      }
    ],
    "params": {
      "output_name": "signed-video.mp4"
    }
  }'

Example response:

{
  "job_id": "01234567-89ab-cdef-0123-456789abcdef",
  "status": "succeeded",
  "results": [
    {
      "input_index": 0,
      "status": "succeeded",
      "output_path": "./.artifacts/output/batch-1/signed-video.mp4"
    }
  ],
  "timing": {
    "queued_at": "2026-04-09T08:00:00Z",
    "started_at": "2026-04-09T08:00:01Z",
    "finished_at": "2026-04-09T08:00:05Z"
  }
}

items.title: Submit An Async Job And Poll It
items.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/v2/c2pa/video \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "inputs": [
      {
        "type": "remote_file_url",
        "url": "https://example.com/video.mp4",
        "filename_hint": "video.mp4"
      }
    ],
    "params": {}
  }'

Response:

{
  "job_id": "01234567-89ab-cdef-0123-456789abcdef",
  "status": "queued"
}

Poll the job:

curl http://localhost:8080/v2/jobs/01234567-89ab-cdef-0123-456789abcdef

items.title: Package A Publication
items.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/v2/sync/package \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "inputs": [
      {
        "type": "local_folder_path",
        "path": "./tests/fixtures/video/mp4"
      }
    ],
    "params": {
      "output_name": "publication"
    }
  }'

items.title: OpenAPI And Scalar
items.text:
• --features openapi exposes /openapi.json
  • --features "openapi scalar-docs" additionally exposes /docs

items.title: S3
items.text:
Build with --features s3 to enable:

  • s3://... remote inputs through remote_file_url and remote_folder_url
  • request-selected S3 outputs through output.type = "s3"

If S3 config is present in config/service.toml but the binary is built without the s3 feature, startup fails.

items.title: ffmpeg Or mp4fragment Not Found
items.text: Install the external tools and ensure they are on PATH.

items.title: Health Check Fails
items.text:
Confirm the service is listening on the configured bind address:

curl -i http://localhost:8080/v2/health

items.title: Sync Request Times Out
items.text: Increase SERVICE__SERVER__SYNC_TIMEOUT_MS or use the async endpoint and poll GET /v2/jobs/{id}.

items.title: Request Body Too Large
items.text: Increase SERVICE__SERVER__MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES if your JSON request, including in_request payloads, exceeds the default limit.

cargo run -p service-http

The default config file is config/service.toml.

items.title: Verify The Service
items.text:
curl -i http://localhost:8080/v2/health

Expected result: 200 OK with an empty body.

items.title: Run A Local Sync Request

curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/v2/sync/c2pa/video \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "inputs": [
      {
        "type": "local_file_path",
        "path": "./tests/fixtures/video/mp4/video1.mp4"
      }
    ],
    "params": {
      "output_name": "signed-video.mp4"
    }

items.title: Outputs

  • The checked-in default is ./.artifacts/output

items.title: Purpose
items.text: Use this flow when you want S3-backed inputs, request-selected S3 outputs, or both.

items.title: Build With The Right Feature
items.text:
cargo run -p service-http --features s3

If S3 config exists but the binary is built without --features s3, startup fails.

items.title: Configure S3 Inputs
items.text:
Enable [input_resolvers.s3] in config/service.toml or by environment override:

export SERVICE__INPUT_RESOLVERS__S3__REGION=eu-central-1
export SERVICE__INPUT_RESOLVERS__S3__BUCKETS=media-bucket,publication-bucket

items.title: Configure S3 Outputs
items.text:
Add or override output_sinks.s3:

export SERVICE__OUTPUT_SINKS__S3__REGION=eu-central-1
export SERVICE__OUTPUT_SINKS__S3__BUCKETS=processed-media,archive-media
export SERVICE__OUTPUT_SINKS__S3__BASE_PREFIX=exports

The requested output.bucket must be in the configured allow-list.

items.title: Example Request
items.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/v2/c2pa/video \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "inputs": [
      {
        "type": "remote_file_url",
        "url": "s3://media-bucket/input/video.mp4",
        "filename_hint": "video.mp4"
      }
    ],
    "params": {
      "output_name": "signed-video.mp4"
    },
    "output": {
      "type": "s3",
      "bucket": "processed-media",
      "prefix": "exports/c2pa"
    }
  }'

items.title: Common Failures
items.text:
• startup error about missing feature: build with --features s3
  • validation failure for output bucket: add the bucket to output_sinks.s3.buckets
  • startup failure for bucket probe: verify credentials, region, endpoint, and bucket permissions

items.title: Health Check Fails
items.text:

  • confirm server.bind_addr
  • test curl -i http://localhost:8080/v2/health

items.title: Sync Requests Time Out
items.text:
• increase SERVICE__SERVER__SYNC_TIMEOUT_MS
  • use async submission and poll GET /v2/jobs/{id}

items.title: Request Body Too Large
items.text:
• increase SERVICE__SERVER__MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES
  • this matters especially for in_request payloads

items.title: S3 Config Causes Startup Failure
items.text:
• build with --features s3
  • verify the configured region, endpoint, and bucket list
  • verify the output buckets are writable at startup

  • run make docs-openapi
  • run make docs-check
  • verify Python can create .venv and install Zensical

items.title: Roadmap
items.text:
This roadmap highlights the next capability areas we expect to document and expand in the C2PA Claim Generator project.

It is a public-facing view of the direction of the docs and service surface, not a dated delivery plan. The topics below are intentionally unordered and should be read as planned capability areas rather than release commitments.

items.title: Current Scope
items.text:
Today, the docs cover the current service surface, runtime configuration, local operation, S3-backed operation, and the main architecture and development workflows.

The roadmap extends that foundation into four areas where the current docs and public surface are intentionally incomplete:

  • Softbinding Insertion: move from placeholder watermark endpoints toward a documented softbinding insertion capability
  • Softbinding Manifest Storage: describe how durable manifest storage could support lookup, retrieval, and operational workflows
  • C2PA Orchestration: document how higher-level orchestration could shape multi-step C2PA flows beyond the current request-processing model
  • Multi-Cloud Deployment Guides: extend the current local and S3-backed operational guidance into broader deployment patterns

items.title: How To Read This Section
items.text:
Each roadmap page follows the same format:

  • a short note on the current repo or docs state
  • the planned outcome
  • why the area matters
  • what likely needs to be in place first
  • the main open questions still shaping the final docs

items.title: Current State
items.text: The current architecture docs already describe job orchestration in the application layer through BatchService, workers, and processor dispatch. What is not yet documented is a future higher-level orchestration story for coordinating richer C2PA-specific workflows across multiple processing steps or capability areas.

items.title: Planned Outcome
items.text:
Document a clearer orchestration model for C2PA workflows that explains how multi-step processing could be composed, coordinated, and surfaced in the service and docs experience.

This roadmap item is about making orchestration a first-class documented concept, not just an internal implementation detail.

items.title: Why It Matters
items.text: As the service grows beyond the current signing and packaging paths, readers will need a clearer explanation of how different C2PA operations relate to one another. A dedicated orchestration story would help align future API behavior, operational expectations, and architecture guidance.

items.title: What Needs To Be In Place
items.text:
• a stable vocabulary for describing orchestration versus individual processing steps
  • clearer boundaries between public workflow concepts and internal worker coordination
  • documentation on how orchestration interacts with sync and async execution models
  • a consistent way to explain future composed flows without fragmenting the public docs

items.title: Open Questions
items.text:
• Which orchestration concepts should be public and which should remain architecture-only?
  • How should future orchestrated workflows be described without duplicating endpoint-level documentation?
  • When new orchestration paths arrive, should they be presented as separate capabilities or as layered guides over the existing API surface?

items.title: Current State
items.text: The current docs explain how to run the service locally and how to enable S3-backed inputs and outputs. They do not yet provide platform-oriented deployment guidance for running the service across a broader set of cloud environments.

items.title: Planned Outcome
items.text:
Document a multi-cloud deployment story that explains how to package, configure, and operate the service across different hosting environments without assuming a single platform path.

The goal is a practical set of deployment guides that help operators move from local or S3-backed experimentation toward repeatable cloud-hosted operation.

items.title: Why It Matters
items.text: Public deployment guidance makes the project easier to evaluate, operate, and integrate into different runtime environments. It also helps separate core service behavior from environment-specific operational choices, which is important for adoption beyond one infrastructure profile.

items.title: What Needs To Be In Place
items.text:
• a clear baseline deployment model for the HTTP service and its external tool dependencies
  • guidance for configuration, secrets, storage, and network exposure in hosted environments
  • documentation boundaries between generic operational requirements and platform-specific examples
  • a repeatable way to explain feature flags and optional components in deployment contexts

items.title: Open Questions
items.text:
• Which deployment concerns should stay generic across platforms, and which need platform-specific runbooks?
  • How much production guidance belongs in the public docs versus environment-specific implementation notes?
  • What is the minimum operational baseline that every cloud deployment guide should share?

items.title: Current State
items.text: The current public HTTP surface exposes watermark endpoints only as placeholders. The API reference and handler quick reference both describe them as reserved for future softbinding work and currently returning 501 Not Implemented.

items.title: Planned Outcome
items.text:
Document a real softbinding insertion capability that explains how clients submit a request, what kind of media and metadata inputs are expected, and what output or job state they should receive in return.

The target is not only a new endpoint description, but a clear public explanation of where softbinding fits within the broader C2PA processing model.

items.title: Why It Matters
items.text: Softbinding is one of the clearest gaps between the current documented API surface and the future direction implied by the placeholder routes. Making it a documented capability would help external readers understand how the project intends to handle watermark-oriented or softbinding-oriented workflows alongside signing and packaging.

items.title: What Needs To Be In Place
items.text:
• a concrete softbinding processing model instead of placeholder route behavior
  • request and response semantics that fit the existing sync and async job conventions
  • documentation on input expectations, output artifacts, and failure modes
  • clarity on how softbinding relates to existing C2PA signing and packaging flows

items.title: Open Questions
items.text:
• Should softbinding be presented as its own primary workflow or as one step within a broader C2PA pipeline?
  • What parts of the future softbinding surface belong in the public HTTP contract versus implementation-specific docs?
  • How closely should the softbinding request model mirror the existing video-signing request patterns?

items.title: Current State
items.text: The current docs do not describe a durable manifest-storage path for softbinding data. The architecture docs mention future store-oriented expansion in the infrastructure layer, but the checked-in public docs remain focused on request processing, output writing, and current runtime paths.

items.title: Planned Outcome
items.text:
Document a database-backed softbinding manifest storage capability that explains how manifest data can be stored, retrieved, and connected to future softbinding workflows.

The intended outcome is a public explanation of durable storage behavior rather than a low-level schema document. Readers should be able to understand why manifest persistence exists, what operational problem it solves, and how it fits into the service model.

items.title: Why It Matters
items.text: Durable manifest storage would turn softbinding from a one-off processing action into a capability that can support later retrieval, operational tracking, and integration with surrounding systems. It also creates a clearer path for documenting future lookup and status workflows beyond immediate processing results.

items.title: What Needs To Be In Place
items.text:
• a clear persistence model for softbinding manifest records
  • documented boundaries between transient job state and durable manifest state
  • retrieval and lookup behavior that fits the public service story
  • operational guidance for running the storage layer in production environments

items.title: Open Questions
items.text:
• What parts of manifest data should be durably stored versus derived on demand?
  • How much of the storage model should appear in public API docs compared with operator-focused documentation?
  • Should manifest storage be described first as a service capability, an integration surface, or an operational dependency?
